tEn: Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
tZh: 通过人类反馈的强化学习训练有益且无害的助手
sEn: RLHF alignment improves NLP performance and is compatible with specialized skills training.
sZh: RLHF对齐训练提升了几乎所有NLP评估的性能，并且与专业技能训练完全兼容。
absEn: We apply preference modeling and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) to finetune language models to act as helpful and harmless assistants. We find this alignment training improves performance on almost all NLP evaluations, and is fully compatible with training for specialized skills such as python coding and summarization. We explore an iterated online mode of training, where preference models and RL policies are updated on a weekly cadence with fresh human feedback data, efficiently improving our datasets and models. Finally, we investigate the robustness of RLHF training, and identify a roughly linear relation between the RL reward and the square root of the KL divergence between the policy and its initialization. Alongside our main results, we perform peripheral analyses on calibration, competing objectives, and the use of OOD detection, compare our models with human writers, and provide samples from our models using prompts appearing in recent related work.
absZh: 我们应用偏好建模和基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）来微调语言模型，使其成为有益且无害的助手。我们发现这种对齐训练几乎在所有NLP评估中都能提升性能，并且与Python编程和摘要等专业技能的训练完全兼容。我们探索了一种迭代在线训练模式，其中偏好模型和RL策略每周根据新的人类反馈数据进行更新，从而高效地改进我们的数据集和模型。最后，我们研究了RLHF训练的鲁棒性，并发现RL奖励与策略及其初始化之间的KL散度的平方根大致呈线性关系。除了主要结果外，我们还进行了关于校准、竞争目标和OOD检测使用的辅助分析，将我们的模型与人类写手进行比较，并提供了使用近期相关工作提示的模型样本。

contrib.en: Applied RLHF to finetune language models as helpful and harmless assistants, improving NLP evaluations.
contrib.zh: 应用基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）微调语言模型，使其成为有用且无害的助手，提升了几乎所有自然语言处理评估的性能。

contrib.en: Demonstrated compatibility of alignment training with specialized skills like Python coding and summarization.
contrib.zh: 证明对齐训练与Python编程和摘要等专业技能完全兼容。

contrib.zh: 探索了迭代在线训练模式，每周更新偏好模型和强化学习策略，有效改进了数据集和模型。

contrib.en: Identified a linear relation between RL reward and square root of KL divergence from initial policy.
contrib.zh: 发现强化学习奖励与策略初始化的KL散度平方根之间存在近似线性关系。

contrib.en: Conducted peripheral analyses on calibration, competing objectives, and OOD detection.
contrib.zh: 对校准、竞争目标和分布外检测进行了外围分析。

contrib.en: Compared models with human writers and provided samples from prompts in recent related work.
contrib.zh: 将模型与人类写手进行比较，并使用近期相关工作提示提供了模型样本。

limits.en: RLHF may not be the best approach for honesty; other techniques may be more effective.
limits.zh: 基于人类反馈的强化学习可能不是实现诚实的最佳方法；其他技术可能更有效。

limits.en: Preference models become less calibrated and robust at higher scores due to data scarcity.
limits.zh: 由于数据稀缺，偏好模型在较高分数下校准性和鲁棒性下降。

limits.en: Online training requires careful handling of distribution shift and entropy reduction.
limits.zh: 在线训练需要谨慎处理分布偏移和熵减少问题。

limits.en: Harmlessness training can lead to over-optimization and reduced helpfulness if not balanced.
limits.zh: 如果未平衡，无害训练可能导致过度优化并降低有用性。

limits.en: Results may not generalize to real-world tasks or adversarial evaluations.
limits.zh: 结果可能无法推广到现实任务或对抗性评估。

items.en: We apply preference modeling and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) to finetune language models to act as helpful and harmless assistants. We find this alignment training improves performance on almost all NLP evaluations, and is fully compatible with training for specialized skills such as python coding and summarization. We explore an iterated online mode of training, where preference models and RL policies are updated on a weekly cadence with fresh human feedback data, efficiently improving our datasets and models. Finally, we investigate the robustness of RLHF training, and identify a roughly linear relation between the RL reward and the square root of the KL divergence between the policy and its initialization. Alongside our main results, we perform peripheral analyses on calibration, competing objectives, and the use of OOD detection, compare our models with human writers, and provide samples from our models using prompts appearing in recent related work.
items.zh: 我们应用偏好建模和基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）来微调语言模型，使其成为有用且无害的助手。我们发现这种对齐训练几乎在所有NLP评估中都能提升性能，并且与Python编程和摘要等专业技能的训练完全兼容。我们探索了一种迭代在线训练模式，其中偏好模型和RL策略每周根据新的人类反馈数据更新，从而高效改进我们的数据集和模型。最后，我们研究了RLHF训练的鲁棒性，并发现RL奖励与策略及其初始化的KL散度的平方根之间存在近似线性关系。除了主要结果，我们还进行了关于校准、竞争目标和OOD检测使用的辅助分析，将我们的模型与人类写作者进行比较，并提供了使用近期相关工作中出现的提示生成的模型样本。

items.en: We would like to develop techniques to train AI agents that are helpful, honest, and harmless [Askell et al., 2021]. In this paper we show that we can train a relatively helpful and harmless111We do not focus explicitly on honesty/truthfulness in this paper, as we believe that techniques other than pure human feedback may be more efficient and effective at training models to be honest. But we certainly believe that honesty is a crucial goal for AI alignment, and our models do improve on evaluations of honesty (see Figure 5). (HH) natural language assistant by collecting human preference data and applying the techniques of preference modeling (PMing) and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). Our full training process is summarized in Figure 2.
items.zh: 我们希望开发技术来训练有用、诚实且无害的AI智能体[Askell et al., 2021]。在本文中，我们展示了通过收集人类偏好数据并应用偏好建模（PMing）和基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）技术，可以训练出一个相对有用且无害的自然语言助手。我们的完整训练过程总结在图2中。

items.capEn: Figure 1: This plot summarizes crowdworker preferences for a variety of models, including context-distilled models, RLHF models trained on our ‘static’ dataset, and RLHF models trained by an iterated ‘online’ method for either helpfulness and harmlessness (HH) or for helpfulness only. We present both Elo scores and a match to the frequency with which crowdworkers prefer samples as compared to the 52B context-distilled model. For both helpfulness and harmlessness, a higher score is more desirable.
items.capZh: 图1：该图总结了众包工作者对多种模型的偏好，包括上下文蒸馏模型、在我们的“静态”数据集上训练的RLHF模型，以及通过迭代“在线”方法训练的RLHF模型（针对有用性和无害性（HH）或仅针对有用性）。我们展示了Elo分数以及众包工作者偏好样本的频率与52B上下文蒸馏模型的比较。对于有用性和无害性，分数越高越理想。

items.en: Our goal is not to define or prescribe what ‘helpful’ and ‘harmless’ mean but to evaluate the effectiveness of our training techniques, so for the most part we simply let our crowdworkers interpret these concepts as they see fit. We treat helpfulness and harmlessness separately, collecting distinct human-preference datasets for each. For helpfulness, we ask crowdworkers to solicit our models to assist with any purely text-based tasks such as answering questions, writing or editing documents, or discussing plans and decisions. For harmlessness, we invite crowdworkers to adversarially probe or ‘red-team’ our language models in order to provoke harmful responses: either to help them with harmful goals, such as planning a bank robbery, or to cause the AI to use toxic language.222We warn crowdworkers that they may encounter upsetting content, and we frequently invite them to cease this task and pursue ‘helpful’ mode instead; we will discuss our approach to red-teaming in a forthcoming publication. At each stage of their conversations with the AI assistant, crowdworkers are presented with two possible responses. Those engaged in the helpfulness task are instructed to choose the more helpful and honest (i.e. better) response. Those engaged in the red teaming task are instructed to choose the more harmful (i.e. worse) response. These conversations and the expressed human preferences form our datasets.333Our helpfulness data is available at https://github.com/anthropics/hh-rlhf, and our harmlessness data will be made available in the future. Our work has benefited from other publicly available alignment-related data, such as for summarization [Stiennon et al., 2020], and we hope that the release of such datasets can be a standard practice for researchers working towards safe and beneficial AI.
items.zh: 我们的目标不是定义或规定“有用”和“无害”的含义，而是评估我们训练技术的有效性，因此在大多数情况下，我们让众包工作者自行解释这些概念。我们将有用性和无害性分开处理，为每个概念收集不同的人类偏好数据集。对于有用性，我们要求众包工作者让我们的模型协助完成任何纯文本任务，例如回答问题、撰写或编辑文档，或讨论计划和决策。对于无害性，我们邀请众包工作者对抗性地探测或“红队测试”我们的语言模型，以引发有害响应：要么帮助他们实现有害目标（如计划银行抢劫），要么导致AI使用有毒语言。我们警告众包工作者可能会遇到令人不安的内容，并经常邀请他们停止此任务并转向“有用”模式；我们将在即将发表的出版物中讨论我们的红队测试方法。在与AI助手的对话的每个阶段，众包工作者会看到两个可能的响应。从事有用性任务的人被指示选择更有用和更诚实（即更好）的响应。从事红队测试任务的人被指示选择更有害（即更差）的响应。这些对话和表达的人类偏好构成了我们的数据集。我们的有用性数据可在https://github.com/anthropics/hh-rlhf获取，无害性数据将在未来提供。我们的工作受益于其他公开可用的对齐相关数据，例如摘要数据[Stiennon et al., 2020]，我们希望此类数据集的发布能成为致力于安全和有益AI的研究人员的标准做法。

items.en: Helpfulness and harmlessness often stand in opposition to each other. An excessive focus on avoiding harm can lead to ‘safe’ responses that don’t actually address the needs of the human. An excessive focus on being helpful can lead to responses that help humans cause harm or generate toxic content. We demonstrate this tension quantitatively by showing that preference models trained to primarily evaluate one of these qualities perform very poorly (much worse than chance) on the other. Fortunately, we find that PMs trained on a mixture of both datasets can nevertheless learn the right lessons and behave helpfully when appropriate, while encouraging the polite refusal of harmful requests. With preference models in hand, we then train helpful and harmless assistants via reinforcement learning, using the PM scores as rewards. We evaluate both PM performance and the more relevant performance characteristics of our RLHF-trained models. As can be seen in Figure 1, purely helpful RLHF-trained models are far easier to red-team, while helpful+harmless models are both very helpful and much less harmful.
items.zh: 有用性和无害性常常相互对立。过度关注避免伤害可能导致“安全”的响应，但实际上并未满足人类的需求。过度关注有用性可能导致帮助人类造成伤害或生成有毒内容的响应。我们通过展示主要评估其中一种质量的偏好模型在另一种质量上表现非常差（远低于随机水平）来定量证明这种张力。幸运的是，我们发现基于两个数据集混合训练的PM仍然可以学到正确的教训，并在适当的时候表现得有用，同时鼓励礼貌地拒绝有害请求。有了偏好模型，我们随后通过强化学习训练有用且无害的助手，使用PM分数作为奖励。我们评估了PM性能以及我们RLHF训练模型的相关性能特征。如图1所示，纯有用性RLHF训练的模型更容易被红队测试，而有用+无害模型既非常有用又更少有害。

items.en: A question that’s often raised about alignment training is whether it will compromise AI capabilities. We find that when RLHF is applied to large language models, the answer seems to be an almost-categorical no. Our RLHF-trained models tend to perform better than their raw, generative counterparts on virtually all evaluations, as summarized in Figure 3. We also argue that one can mix specialized skills with alignment-related training without compromising either alignment or performance. In practice, aligned models are likely to be more user-friendly and deployable than their raw counterparts, which suggests that there’s little reason to deploy models that have not been finetuned for alignment.
items.zh: 关于对齐训练的一个常见问题是它是否会损害AI能力。我们发现，当RLHF应用于大型语言模型时，答案似乎几乎是否定的。我们的RLHF训练模型在几乎所有评估中往往比其原始生成对应模型表现更好，如图3总结所示。我们还认为，可以将专业技能与对齐相关训练混合，而不会损害对齐或性能。在实践中，对齐模型可能比原始模型更用户友好且更易部署，这表明几乎没有理由部署未经过对齐微调的模型。

items.capEn: Figure 2: This diagram summarizes our data collection and model training workflow.
items.capZh: 图2：该图总结了我们的数据收集和模型训练工作流程。

items.en: We collect separate helpfulness and harmlessness (i.e. red-teaming) datasets primarily using various 52B language models (see Section 2 for details) in our interface (Figure 6). Crowdworkers have open-ended conversations with the models, either soliciting help, or providing instructions, or attempting to get the model to emit harmful responses, and they are asked to choose the more helpful response or the more harmful444This means that our helpfulness dataset goes ‘up’ in desirability during the conversation, while our harmlessness dataset goes ‘down’ in desirability. We chose the latter to thoroughly explore bad behavior, but it is likely not ideal for teaching good behavior. We believe this difference in our data distributions creates subtle problems for RLHF, and suggest that others who want to use RLHF to train safer models consider the analysis in Section 4.4. response at each conversational step, respectively.
items.zh: 我们主要使用各种52B语言模型（详见第2节）在我们的界面（图6）中收集独立的有用性和无害性（即红队测试）数据集。众包工作者与模型进行开放式对话，要么寻求帮助，要么提供指令，要么试图让模型发出有害响应，他们被要求在每个对话步骤中选择更有用的响应或更有害的响应。

items.en: We collect three tranches of data, one from our initial models, one with rejection sampling against early preference models, and a final dataset gathered with models trained with ‘online’ reinforcement learning from human feedback, which we improve on a roughly weekly cadence. See Section 2.3.
items.zh: 我们收集了三批数据：第一批来自我们的初始模型，第二批通过针对早期偏好模型的拒绝采样获得，第三批使用通过“在线”强化学习从人类反馈训练的模型收集，我们大约每周改进一次。详见第2.3节。

items.en: Smaller models experience severe ‘alignment taxes’ – their performance on a wide variety of evaluations declines after RLHF training. However, we find a variety of alignment bonuses, with our 13B and 52B555Incidentally, this means that alignment work focusing only on smaller models could lead to erroneous conclusions if naively extrapolated to larger models. RLHF-trained models performing better at zero-shot NLP evaluations, and the same at few-shot evaluations.
items.zh: 较小的模型会经历严重的“对齐税”——它们在各种评估上的性能在RLHF训练后下降。然而，我们发现多种对齐红利，我们的13B和52B RLHF训练模型在零样本NLP评估中表现更好，在少样本评估中表现相同。

items.en: Natural language RLHF training for HH can be applied to models that have been first finetuned on code, and it improves their programming ability on evaluations (presumably by improving general-purpose instruction following). We also find that mixing preference model training for HH with the specialized skill of summarization [Stiennon et al., 2020] incurs no degradation in performance in either HH or summarization. So there is no reason not to combine alignment training with more specific, valuable skills.
items.zh: 针对HH的自然语言RLHF训练可以应用于首先在代码上微调的模型，并且它提高了它们在评估中的编程能力（大概是通过改进通用指令遵循）。我们还发现，将HH的偏好模型训练与摘要[Stiennon et al., 2020]等专业技能混合，不会导致HH或摘要性能下降。因此，没有理由不将对齐训练与更具体、有价值的技能结合起来。

items.en: There is a tension between helpfulness and harmlessness, which can be measured at the level of both preference modeling and RLHF-trained policies (Figure 1). However, as model size increases, PMs perform better on both distributions simultaneously and become much more robust to the relative proportions of helpful and harmless training data.
items.zh: 有用性和无害性之间存在张力，这可以在偏好建模和RLHF训练策略的层面上测量（图1）。然而，随着模型规模的增加，PM同时在两个分布上表现更好，并且对有用和无害训练数据的相对比例变得更加鲁棒。

items.en: We also show that one can use OOD detection techniques [Fort et al., 2021] to reject most strange and harmful requests (Figure 22), with little or no harmful examples (Figure 23).
items.zh: 我们还展示了可以使用OOD检测技术[Fort et al., 2021]来拒绝大多数奇怪和有害的请求（图22），几乎没有有害示例（图23）。

items.capEn: Figure 3: RLHF model performance on zero-shot and few-shot NLP tasks. For each model size, we plot the mean accuracy on MMMLU, Lambada, HellaSwag, OpenBookQA, ARC-Easy, ARC-Challenge, and TriviaQA. On zero-shot tasks, RLHF training for helpfulness and harmlessness hurts performance for small models, but actually improves performance for larger models. Full results for each task are given in Figure 28 (zero-shot) and Figure 29 (few-shot).
items.capZh: 图3：RLHF模型在零样本和少样本NLP任务上的性能。对于每个模型大小，我们绘制了MMMLU、Lambada、HellaSwag、OpenBookQA、ARC-Easy、ARC-Challenge和TriviaQA的平均准确率。在零样本任务上，针对有用性和无害性的RLHF训练会损害小模型的性能，但实际上提高了大模型的性能。每个任务的完整结果见图28（零样本）和图29（少样本）。

items.en: We study scaling relations for PM accuracy as a function of model and dataset size, and find roughly log-linear trends (Figure 7), though we encounter some idiosyncrasies (Figures 31 and 32).
items.zh: 我们研究了PM准确率随模型和数据集大小的缩放关系，并发现了大致对数线性的趋势（图7），尽管我们遇到了一些特殊情况（图31和图32）。

items.en: We conduct experiments on the robustness of RLHF (see Figure 4), where we split our datasets in half and train separate preference models on each half. Then we train RL models against one PM while evaluating with the other. We conclude that larger PMs are more robust than smaller PMs, and as expected, overfitting increases during RLHF training.
items.zh: 我们对RLHF的鲁棒性进行了实验（见图4），我们将数据集分成两半，并在每一半上训练单独的偏好模型。然后，我们针对一个PM训练RL模型，同时用另一个PM进行评估。我们得出结论，较大的PM比较小的PM更鲁棒，并且正如预期的那样，过拟合在RLHF训练期间增加。

items.en: We find that DKL(π||π0)\sqrt{D_{\rm KL}(\pi||\pi_{0})} and reward are approximately linearly related for much of RLHF training (see Figures 4 and 13), where π𝜋\pi and π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} are the policy and initial policy, respectively. We explain how this relation may arise and discuss possible applications and future directions.
items.zh: 我们发现DKL(π||π0)和奖励在RLHF训练的大部分过程中近似线性相关（见图4和图13），其中π和π0分别是策略和初始策略。我们解释了这种关系可能如何产生，并讨论了可能的应用和未来方向。

items.en: We study iterated online training, where we update our preference models and RLHF policies on a weekly cadence, and then re-deploy these fresh RLHF models to interact with crowdworkers. This significantly improved our models as evaluated by crowdworkers (Figure 1), and greatly improved our dataset as judged by our own PMs (Figure 15), filling out the upper tail in terms of quality. To remove confounders and bolster our conclusions, we perform additional controlled experiments (Figure 16) holding dataset size and other hyperparameters fixed.
items.zh: 我们研究了迭代在线训练，其中我们每周更新偏好模型和RLHF策略，然后重新部署这些新的RLHF模型与众包工作者交互。这显著改进了我们的模型（如图1所示），并大大改进了我们的数据集（如图15所示），在质量上填充了上尾。为了消除混淆因素并加强我们的结论，我们进行了额外的控制实验（图16），保持数据集大小和其他超参数固定。

items.capEn: Figure 4: This figure shows results from RL robustness experiments. We split our static dataset 50:50, and trained separate PMs on each half, which we refer to as train PMs and test PMs. We then trained RLHF policies against the train PMs, while evaluating their score with respect to the test PMs. Overfitting can then be observed as a divergence between the train and test PM scores. (left) We see that training is quite robust up to about 150k training samples, but beyond that point the train and test PM’s disagree, with the train PM assigning a higher mean reward. We also show an approximately linear relationship between PM score gain and the square root of the KL divergence (between the policy and its initial snapshot) during early phase of training—we observe this for all our RLHF runs, as discussed more in Section 4.3. (right) This shows similar results for various policy sizes, all trained and tested on 52B PMs.
items.capZh: 图4：该图显示了RL鲁棒性实验的结果。我们将静态数据集50:50分割，并在每一半上训练单独的PM，分别称为训练PM和测试PM。然后，我们针对训练PM训练RLHF策略，同时评估它们相对于测试PM的分数。过拟合可以观察为训练和测试PM分数之间的差异。（左）我们看到训练在约150k训练样本之前相当鲁棒，但超过该点后，训练和测试PM出现分歧，训练PM分配更高的平均奖励。我们还展示了在训练早期阶段，PM分数增益与KL散度平方根（策略与其初始快照之间）之间的近似线性关系——我们在所有RLHF运行中都观察到了这一点，详见第4.3节。（右）这显示了不同策略大小的类似结果，所有策略都在52B PM上训练和测试。

items.en: NLP and Code Evaluations: We evaluate our models on MMLU [Hendrycks et al., 2021b], Lambada [Paperno et al., 2016], Hellaswag [Zellers et al., 2019], OpenBookQA [Mihaylov et al., 2018], ARC [Clark et al., 2018], and TriviaQA [Joshi et al., 2017]; see Figures 28 and 29 for full results and Figure 3 for the mean. In every case except for TriviaQA, 12B and 52B RLHF-trained models perform better than base LMs. Separately, we take Python coding models and finetune them with natural language RLHF, and then evaluate them on the codex HumanEval [Chen et al., 2021]; see Figure 21. We also experiment with mixing PM training for HH with summarization [Stiennon et al., 2020] as a specialized skill, and evaluate the resulting PM performance (Figure 20), finding that mixed training does not degrade PM accuracies.
items.zh: NLP和代码评估：我们在MMLU [Hendrycks et al., 2021b]、Lambada [Paperno et al., 2016]、Hellaswag [Zellers et al., 2019]、OpenBookQA [Mihaylov et al., 2018]、ARC [Clark et al., 2018]和TriviaQA [Joshi et al., 2017]上评估我们的模型；完整结果见图28和图29，平均值见图3。除TriviaQA外，在所有情况下，12B和52B RLHF训练模型的表现都优于基础LM。另外，我们取Python编码模型并用自然语言RLHF进行微调，然后在codex HumanEval [Chen et al., 2021]上评估它们；见图21。我们还尝试将HH的PM训练与摘要[Stiennon et al., 2020]作为专业技能混合，并评估所得PM性能（图20），发现混合训练不会降低PM准确率。

items.en: Static Alignment Evaluations: We evaluate our PMs using our HHH Evaluations [Askell et al., 2021] from BIG-Bench666https://github.com/google/BIG-bench (Figure 5), on Bot Adversarial Dialogues [Xu et al., 2020], and for gender bias [Rae et al., 2021] (Figure 12). We evaluate our RLHF models on TruthfulQA [Lin et al., 2021] (Figure 5), BBQ-Lite [Parrish et al., 2021] from BIG-Bench, gender bias (Figure 40), and sentiment based on race and religion [Rae et al., 2021] (Figure 17). RLHF improves sentiment towards all groups, but does not remove bias.
items.zh: 静态对齐评估：我们使用BIG-Bench中的HHH评估[Askell等人，2021]（图5）、Bot对抗对话[Xu等人，2020]以及性别偏见[Rae等人，2021]（图12）来评估我们的偏好模型。我们在TruthfulQA[Lin等人，2021]（图5）、BIG-Bench中的BBQ-Lite[Parrish等人，2021]、性别偏见（图40）以及基于种族和宗教的情感[Rae等人，2021]（图17）上评估我们的RLHF模型。RLHF改善了对所有群体的情感，但并未消除偏见。

items.en: Human Evaluations: We compute Elo scores based on the preferences of our crowdworkers, comparing context-distilled models, base RLHF trained models, and final online RLHF models (Figure 1). We also test our online models’ performance during training (Figure 15), compare various levels of rejection sampling (Figure 36), and perform a controlled experiment on iterated online training (Figure 16). Furthermore, we hired professional writers to compose conversations where an assistant provides high-quality, helpful and honest responses, and we then asked crowdworkers to compare our model’s responses to those of these writers. Crowdworkers prefer our online HH model to these writers777This finding should be interpreted with caution; we do not believe it is necessarily representative of performance on real-world tasks, and this evaluation was not adversarial. about 57% of the time.
items.zh: 人工评估：我们基于众包工人的偏好计算Elo分数，比较了上下文蒸馏模型、基础RLHF训练模型和最终在线RLHF模型（图1）。我们还测试了在线模型在训练过程中的表现（图15），比较了不同级别的拒绝采样（图36），并对迭代在线训练进行了对照实验（图16）。此外，我们聘请专业作家编写对话，其中助手提供高质量、有用且诚实的回答，然后让众包工人将我们模型的回答与这些作家的回答进行比较。众包工人在约57%的情况下更喜欢我们的在线HH模型而非这些作家。

items.en: Samples: We provide samples from all of the PALMs [Solaiman and Dennison, 2021] sensitive questions and from prompts provided with InstructGPT [Ouyang et al., 2022] and LaMDA [Thoppilan et al., 2022] in Appendix C. We show some comparisons with human writers in Section 6.1, and we show several short dialogues in Section 6.3. To mitigate the problem of cherry picking samples, we generate 17 samples per prompt and display only the median sample, as ranked by our online HH preference model.
items.zh: 样本：我们在附录C中提供了来自所有PALM[Solaiman和Dennison，2021]敏感问题以及InstructGPT[Ouyang等人，2022]和LaMDA[Thoppilan等人，2022]提供的提示的样本。我们在第6.1节中展示了一些与人类作家的比较，并在第6.3节中展示了一些简短对话。为了缓解挑选样本的问题，我们为每个提示生成17个样本，并仅显示中位数样本（根据我们的在线HH偏好模型排序）。

items.capEn: Figure 5: (left) Here we show accuracy on the HHH alignment evaluation dataset we constructed previously [Askell et al., 2021] and shared on BIG-Bench. We see that our static preference models greatly outperform plain language models, including context distilled HHH models. This confirms that the data generated by our crowdworkers has taught preference models the desired lessons. (right) Our RLHF training improves performance on TruthfulQA (MC1) [Lin et al., 2021] for large models, with an effect that increases with model size. These RLHF models were trained from our static dataset (i.e. they did not use online data).
items.capZh: 图5：（左）我们展示了在之前构建的HHH对齐评估数据集[Askell等人，2021]（已在BIG-Bench上共享）上的准确率。我们看到，我们的静态偏好模型远优于普通语言模型，包括上下文蒸馏的HHH模型。这证实了我们的众包工人生成的数据教会了偏好模型所需的经验。（右）我们的RLHF训练提高了大型模型在TruthfulQA（MC1）[Lin等人，2021]上的表现，且效果随模型规模增大而增强。这些RLHF模型是从我们的静态数据集训练的（即未使用在线数据）。

items.en: Two recent papers, LaMDA [Thoppilan et al., 2022] and InstructGPT [Ouyang et al., 2022] have particular similarity to this work. Both use human data to train large language models to be more generally useful or aligned. Both use language models somewhat larger than our 52B model.
items.zh: 最近的两篇论文，LaMDA[Thoppilan等人，2022]和InstructGPT[Ouyang等人，2022]，与本研究特别相似。两者都使用人类数据来训练大型语言模型，使其更具通用性或更对齐。两者使用的语言模型都比我们的52B模型稍大。

items.en: LaMDA [Thoppilan et al., 2022] finetunes large language models to participate in interesting, helpful, factually grounded, and safe natural language dialogue. As with our work, they include notions of both positive interactions and safety/harmlessness. And their use of external information to ensure accuracy/groundedness goes beyond the methods we discuss here, and is perhaps more similar to WebGPT and GopherCite [Nakano et al., 2021, Menick et al., 2022]. However, some differences are that rather than using reinforcement learning, they apply a mix of supervised learning techniques (both generative and discriminative), and their data collection process involves absolute ratings rather than comparisons. They do not explore whether their methods impose an ‘alignment tax’ on capabilities.
items.zh: LaMDA[Thoppilan等人，2022]微调大型语言模型以参与有趣、有用、基于事实且安全的自然语言对话。与我们的工作一样，它们包含了积极互动和安全/无害的概念。它们使用外部信息来确保准确性/基于事实，这超出了我们在此讨论的方法，并且可能更类似于WebGPT和GopherCite[Nakano等人，2021，Menick等人，2022]。然而，一些不同之处在于，它们没有使用强化学习，而是应用了监督学习技术的混合（包括生成式和判别式），并且它们的数据收集过程涉及绝对评分而非比较。它们没有探讨其方法是否对能力施加了“对齐税”。

items.en: InstructGPT [Ouyang et al., 2022] finetunes GPT-3-type models [Brown et al., 2020] to improve their helpfulness. As in this work, they use reinforcement learning from human preferences, as expressed through comparisons. However, they also include a supervised learning stage of training, whereas in contrast our finetuning occurs purely through RL (we perform context distillation, but this is much more like simple prompting). Perhaps the main contrast with our work is that they do not include harmlessness training, or explore tensions between helpfulness and harmlessness. Their approach also differs from ours in some details: they did not train preference models larger than 6B parameters, and they mixed pretraining with RL in order to avoid a degradation in evaluation performance.
items.zh: InstructGPT[Ouyang等人，2022]微调GPT-3类型模型[Brown等人，2020]以提高其有用性。与本研究一样，它们使用基于人类偏好的强化学习（通过比较表达）。然而，它们还包括一个监督学习训练阶段，而我们的微调纯粹通过RL进行（我们执行上下文蒸馏，但这更像简单的提示）。与我们的工作相比，主要区别可能在于它们没有包括无害训练，也没有探索有用性和无害性之间的张力。它们的方法在一些细节上也与我们的不同：它们没有训练超过6B参数的偏好模型，并且它们将预训练与RL混合以避免评估性能下降。

items.en: Our work differs from both InstructGPT and LaMDA in that we explore ‘online’ training, where we update the models interacting with crowdworkers in order to obtain progressively higher-quality data and fill out the tails of our data distribution. Another difference is our exploration of specialized skills such as summarization and coding, which we use to bolster the argument that alignment can be achieved without limiting capabilities. We also explicitly study the tension between helpfulness and harmlessness, which has not been addressed before as far as we are aware. Finally, we explore scaling and robustness in much more detail, including during RL training. With that said, our procedures (Figure 2) are actually somewhat simpler than those employed in these other works. We believe the only essential steps are human feedback data collection, preference modeling, and RLHF training.
items.zh: 我们的工作与InstructGPT和LaMDA的不同之处在于，我们探索了“在线”训练，即更新与众包工人交互的模型，以获得逐步更高质量的数据并填补数据分布的尾部。另一个区别是我们探索了专门技能，如摘要和编码，用于支持对齐可以在不限制能力的情况下实现的论点。我们还明确研究了有用性和无害性之间的张力，据我们所知，这之前未被解决。最后，我们更详细地探索了缩放和鲁棒性，包括在RL训练期间。尽管如此，我们的流程（图2）实际上比这些其他工作中采用的更简单。我们认为唯一必要的步骤是人类反馈数据收集、偏好建模和RLHF训练。

items.en: Several other recent works focus on aspects of truthfulness using retrieval [Lewis et al., 2020, Guu et al., 2020, Borgeaud et al., 2021] from a database, or via internet search and human feedback, such as WebGPT [Nakano et al., 2021] and GopherCite [Menick et al., 2022]. These works are exciting and complementary to our work; in particular our results suggest that their techniques should be very compatible with training for helpfulness and harmlessness. While these works improve the faithful representation of explicit evidence, more work will likely be necessary to achieve honest self-representation from AI systems. We are generally hopeful that techniques independent of human feedback may be applicable to this problem, since a great many sources of truth are not based on human judgment.
items.zh: 其他几项近期工作专注于使用检索[Lewis等人，2020，Guu等人，2020，Borgeaud等人，2021]从数据库或通过互联网搜索和人类反馈来提高真实性，例如WebGPT[Nakano等人，2021]和GopherCite[Menick等人，2022]。这些工作令人兴奋且与我们的工作互补；特别是，我们的结果表明它们的技术应该与训练有用性和无害性非常兼容。虽然这些工作改进了对明确证据的忠实表示，但可能还需要更多工作来实现AI系统的诚实自我表示。我们普遍希望独立于人类反馈的技术可能适用于这个问题，因为许多真理来源并非基于人类判断。

items.en: Safety and ethical issues associated with language models have been extensively discussed (e.g. [Henderson et al., 2017, Bender et al., 2021, Weidinger et al., 2021]), with well-known issues including toxicity, bias, and the possibility that models may reveal personally identifiable information. As models become increasingly powerful, new and surprising capabilities and safety issues may arise [Ganguli et al., 2022]. Other works have explored methods to mitigate these problems (e.g. [Liu et al., 2021, Xu et al., 2020]). Models have also been trained to directly evaluate ethical dilemmas [Jiang et al., 2021], demonstrating improvement on ethics benchmarks [Hendrycks et al., 2021a]. More general research proposals for AI safety include [Amodei et al., 2016, Hendrycks et al., 2021c]. The RL robustness failures we discuss can be viewed as an instance of ‘reward hacking’, which was recently explored in [Pan et al., 2022]. RL policies could also fail to generalize out of distribution in other dangerous ways [Koch et al., 2021].
items.zh: 与语言模型相关的安全和伦理问题已被广泛讨论（例如[Henderson等人，2017，Bender等人，2021，Weidinger等人，2021]），已知问题包括毒性、偏见以及模型可能泄露个人身份信息的可能性。随着模型变得越来越强大，新的和令人惊讶的能力和安全问题可能会出现[Ganguli等人，2022]。其他工作探索了缓解这些问题的方法（例如[Liu等人，2021，Xu等人，2020]）。模型也被训练直接评估伦理困境[Jiang等人，2021]，在伦理基准上表现出改进[Hendrycks等人，2021a]。更一般的AI安全研究提案包括[Amodei等人，2016，Hendrycks等人，2021c]。我们讨论的RL鲁棒性失败可以被视为“奖励黑客”的一个实例，最近在[Pan等人，2022]中进行了探索。RL策略也可能以其他危险方式无法泛化到分布外[Koch等人，2021]。

items.en: Our interest in studying trends with model size is motivated by neural scaling laws [Hestness et al., 2019, Rosenfeld et al., 2019, Kaplan et al., 2020]. A related observation is that as parameter counts grow, models finetune more effectively [Hernandez et al., 2021] and become much less vulnerable to ‘catastrophic forgetting’ [Ramasesh et al., 2022]. We expect this effect helps to explain why our HH training is compatible with good evaluation performance and specialized skills for large models.
items.zh: 我们研究模型规模趋势的兴趣源于神经缩放定律[Hestness等人，2019，Rosenfeld等人，2019，Kaplan等人，2020]。一个相关的观察是，随着参数数量的增长，模型微调更有效[Hernandez等人，2021]，并且变得不那么容易受到“灾难性遗忘”的影响[Ramasesh等人，2022]。我们预计这一效应有助于解释为什么我们的HH训练与大型模型的良好评估性能和专门技能兼容。

items.capEn: Figure 6: We show the interface that crowdworkers use to interact with our models. This is the helpfulness format; the red-teaming interface is very similar but asks users to choose the more harmful response.
items.capZh: 图6：我们展示了众包工人与我们的模型交互的界面。这是有用性格式；红队界面非常相似，但要求用户选择更有害的回答。

items.en: We expect human feedback (HF) to have the largest comparative advantage over other techniques when people have complex intuitions that are easy to elicit but difficult to formalize and automate. This means that when collecting HF, we should try to choose tasks that are as intuitive and familiar as possible. We chose to use natural language dialogue both for these reasons, and because it is so general – essentially any text-based task can be enacted through dialogue, perhaps with some source materials included in-line.
items.zh: 我们预计人类反馈（HF）在其他技术中具有最大的比较优势，当人们拥有复杂直觉时，这些直觉易于引出但难以形式化和自动化。这意味着在收集HF时，我们应该尽量选择尽可能直观和熟悉的任务。我们选择使用自然语言对话既出于这些原因，也因为它非常通用——基本上任何基于文本的任务都可以通过对话实现，也许可以内联包含一些源材料。

items.en: Our human feedback interface can be seen in Figure 6 (for more details see Appendix D). People can interact with our models in natural language via chat, and ask for help with any text-based task. When it’s the model’s conversational turn, users see two possible model responses, and choose one with which to proceed. These two responses may come from the same model, or two different models. They can then ask follow-up questions or provide further instructions to the models. So there are two core components to the task, which repeat several times in each dialogue:
items.zh: 我们的人类反馈界面如图6所示（更多细节见附录D）。人们可以通过聊天以自然语言与我们的模型交互，并请求任何基于文本的任务的帮助。当轮到模型对话时，用户会看到两个可能的模型回答，并选择一个继续。这两个回答可能来自同一个模型，也可能来自两个不同的模型。然后他们可以向模型提出后续问题或提供进一步指示。因此，任务有两个核心组成部分，在每个对话中重复多次：

items.en: Crowdworkers write a chat message to our models, asking them to perform a task, answer a question, or discuss any topic of interest.
items.zh: 众包工人向我们的模型写一条聊天消息，要求它们执行任务、回答问题或讨论任何感兴趣的话题。

items.en: Crowdworkers are shown two responses, and are asked to choose the more helpful and honest response (or in the case of red-teaming, to choose the more harmful response).
items.zh: 众包工人看到两个回答，并被要求选择更有用和更诚实的回答（或者在红队的情况下，选择更有害的回答）。

items.en: We conjectured that crowdworkers who wrote well and engaged the AI in more interesting discussions would tend to have better judgment about which AI responses were most ‘helpful’ and ‘harmless’. This meant that rather than attempting to filter crowdworkers based on label quality, we instead used spot-checks of their writing, which were simpler and more intuitive for us to perform.
items.zh: 我们推测，那些写作良好并与AI进行更有趣讨论的众包工人往往对哪些AI回答最“有用”和“无害”有更好的判断。这意味着我们不是试图基于标签质量过滤众包工人，而是对他们的写作进行抽查，这对我们来说更简单和直观。

items.en: Otherwise, our approach to data collection was to largely let crowdworkers use their own intuitions to define ‘helpfulness’ and ‘harmfulness’. Our hope was that data diversity (which we expect is very valuable) and the ‘wisdom of the crowd’ would provide comparable RoI to a smaller dataset that was more intensively validated and filtered. Overall, our process was roughly of this form:
items.zh: 否则，我们的数据收集方法是让众包工人主要使用自己的直觉来定义“有用性”和“有害性”。我们希望数据多样性（我们预计非常有价值）和“群体智慧”能够提供与更小但更密集验证和过滤的数据集相当的投资回报率。总体而言，我们的过程大致如下：

items.en: We invited master-qualified US-based888We experimented with the general and international MTurk worker population but we observed that data quality was considerably lower (based on spot-checks, but we did not perform a systematic study). MTurk workers to engage in dialogues with our models.
items.zh: 我们邀请了具有大师资格的美国MTurk工人与我们的模型进行对话。

items.en: Rather than evaluating all of our crowdworkers, we identified those who were most prolific, and together accounted for about 80% of our data (roughly 20 crowdworkers). We then evaluated their performance based primarily on the sophistication and variation in their dialogues, as this was quite easy to evaluate intuitively (rather than based on any measure of agreement on helpful/harmless choices). Based on this method, we collected a list of ‘select’ MTurk workers999We also banned a small number who were providing very low-quality data. whom we continued to work with throughout the research process.
items.zh: 我们没有评估所有众包工人，而是确定了那些最多产的工人，他们共同贡献了约80%的数据（大约20名众包工人）。然后，我们主要根据他们对话的复杂性和多样性来评估他们的表现，因为这很容易直观评估（而不是基于任何关于有用/无害选择的一致度量）。基于这种方法，我们收集了一份“精选”MTurk工人名单，并在整个研究过程中继续与他们合作。

items.en: We invited our select crowdworkers to a Slack channel and corresponded with them by email, to ensure that they were being compensated fairly101010For example, crowdworkers alerted us to the fact that interactions with our rejection-sampling models were slower, and thus we increased pay accordingly. and to allow them to alert us to any problems or issues.
items.zh: 我们将选定的众包工作者邀请到一个 Slack 频道，并通过电子邮件与他们联系，以确保他们获得公平的报酬101010例如，众包工作者提醒我们，与我们的拒绝采样模型交互速度较慢，因此我们相应提高了报酬。，并让他们能够向我们报告任何问题或事项。

items.en: We also hired crowdworkers on Upwork, and vetted them in a similar, lightweight way. We have continued to use both platforms throughout this work. We find that it is easier to incentivize very high-quality interactions on platforms such as Upwork, where crowdworkers can easily be paid by the hour, rather than per task. But conversely, MTurk workers tend to generate data much more rapidly, and account for about 80% of our datasets.
items.zh: 我们还在 Upwork 上雇佣了众包工作者，并以类似的轻量级方式对他们进行筛选。在整个工作中，我们持续使用这两个平台。我们发现，在 Upwork 等平台上更容易激励高质量的交互，因为众包工作者可以按小时计酬，而不是按任务计酬。但相反，MTurk 工作者往往能更快地生成数据，约占我们数据集的 80%。

items.en: We did not filter workers based on agreement or other direct measures of label quality, though we evaluated them retrospectively (see Figure 10 right) and found poor average agreement (about 63%) between Anthropic researchers and our crowdworkers, as compared to recent similar work such as [Stiennon et al., 2020, Ouyang et al., 2022].
items.zh: 我们没有根据一致性或其他标签质量的直接度量来筛选工作者，尽管我们事后进行了评估（见图 10 右），并发现 Anthropic 研究人员与我们的众包工作者之间的平均一致性较差（约 63%），与近期类似工作如 [Stiennon et al., 2020, Ouyang et al., 2022] 相比。

items.en: As an important caveat, our crowdworker distribution was not held fixed throughout this work, and we expect that crowdworker quality probably improved as the project went on. We mention this as a possible complication when evaluating the success of the ‘online training’ program discussed in Section 4.5. Conversely, however, since we generally discouraged repetition, crowdworkers who have performed the task many times might also have had a tendency to engage in more esoteric interactions.
items.zh: 一个重要的注意事项是，我们的众包工作者分布在整个工作中并非固定不变，我们预计随着项目的进行，众包工作者的质量可能有所提高。我们提到这一点，作为评估第 4.5 节讨论的“在线训练”计划成功与否时可能出现的复杂因素。但相反，由于我们通常不鼓励重复，多次执行任务的工作者可能倾向于进行更晦涩的交互。

items.en: We should also note that we explicitly told crowdworkers that ‘lying isn’t helpful’ and that they should try to only reward helpful and honest responses, which presumably explains why our models improve somewhat in terms of honesty. That said, we did not expect crowdworkers to fact-check our models significantly, and for example they often prefer responses that include non-functional URLs, which are probably one of the simplest possible ‘lies’ to debunk.
items.zh: 我们还应注意，我们明确告诉众包工作者“撒谎没有帮助”，他们应只奖励有帮助且诚实的回答，这大概解释了为什么我们的模型在诚实性方面有所改善。话虽如此，我们并未期望众包工作者对模型进行大量事实核查，例如，他们通常更喜欢包含无效 URL 的回答，这可能是最简单的“谎言”之一。

full.secZh: 2.2 有用性与无害性（红队）数据集

items.en: We collected two separate datasets using slightly different versions of our interface. For the helpfulness dataset, we asked crowdworkers to have open-ended conversations with our models, asking for help, advice, or for the model to accomplish a task (see Appendix D.2), and to choose the model response that was more helpful. For the harmlessness or red-teaming dataset, we asked crowdworkers to attempt to elicit harmful responses from our models, and to choose the more harmful response offered by the models.
items.zh: 我们使用略有不同版本的界面收集了两个独立的数据集。对于有用性数据集，我们要求众包工作者与我们的模型进行开放式对话，寻求帮助、建议或让模型完成任务（见附录 D.2），并选择更有帮助的模型回答。对于无害性或红队测试数据集，我们要求众包工作者尝试诱导模型产生有害回答，并选择模型提供的更有害的回答。

items.en: Our interface (Figure 6) allows users to express a preference strength. We only include comparisons in our datasets if crowdworkers expressed a preference stronger than the weakest available. In this work we will not otherwise use this preference-strength information; we treat all comparisons in our dataset as binary and of equal weight (so in particular we do not include ties).
items.zh: 我们的界面（图 6）允许用户表达偏好强度。我们仅在众包工作者表达出强于最弱选项的偏好时，才将比较纳入数据集。在本文中，我们不会进一步使用偏好强度信息；我们将数据集中的所有比较视为二元的且权重相等（因此特别地，我们不包含平局）。

items.en: Note that this means our helpfulness dataset tends to move conversations in a more beneficial direction, while in our red-teaming dataset user responses move conversations in a more harmful direction. We made this choice to make it possible for users to fully trick and exploit models while red-teaming, as this was most natural for other work we’re doing that’s specifically focused on harmfulness. However, we believe this difference made it difficult to train models that were both helpful and harmless, as explained in Section 4.4. We plan to remedy this in future work, and would recommend others who are focused on training harmless dialogue models to collect data where users primarily choose model responses that move the conversation in the more beneficial direction instead.
items.zh: 请注意，这意味着我们的有用性数据集倾向于将对话推向更有益的方向，而红队测试数据集中用户的回答则将对话推向更有害的方向。我们做出这一选择是为了让用户在红队测试中能够完全欺骗和利用模型，因为这对于我们其他专门关注有害性的工作最为自然。然而，我们认为这一差异使得训练既有用又无害的模型变得困难，如第 4.4 节所述。我们计划在未来的工作中解决这一问题，并建议其他专注于训练无害对话模型的研究者收集数据时，让用户主要选择将对话推向更有益方向的模型回答。

full.sec: 2.3 Models Deployed to the Feedback Interface and Associated Data Distributions
full.secZh: 2.3 部署到反馈接口的模型及相关数据分布

items.en: For data collection we predominantly111111A very small minority of the data includes samples from smaller models, as the model comparison data described in Section 2.4 is included in our training data, and we did some comparisons across model size. used 52B language models with the broad specifications given in [Askell et al., 2021]. We used three classes of models in our interface:
items.zh: 对于数据收集，我们主要111111极少数数据包含来自较小模型的样本，因为第 2.4 节描述的模型比较数据包含在我们的训练数据中，并且我们进行了一些跨模型大小的比较。使用了 [Askell et al., 2021] 中给出的广泛规格的 52B 语言模型。我们在界面中使用了三类模型：

items.en: HHH Context-Distilled 52B Language Model: At the beginning of the project this was the only model available. It performs similarly to a plain 52B language model prompted with HHH dialogues [Askell et al., 2021].
items.zh: HHH 上下文蒸馏 52B 语言模型：项目开始时这是唯一可用的模型。其性能与使用 HHH 对话提示的普通 52B 语言模型相似 [Askell et al., 2021]。

items.en: Rejection Sampling (RS) with a 52B preference model, where samples were generated from a 52B context-distilled LM. In this case the number k𝑘k of samples was a parameter, but most often we used k=16𝑘16k=16.
items.zh: 使用 52B 偏好模型的拒绝采样（RS），其中样本从 52B 上下文蒸馏 LM 生成。在这种情况下，样本数量 k 是一个参数，但最常用的是 k=16。

items.en: RLHF-Finetuned Models: We used a succession of these models in our interface. The models varied primarily based on the amount of data available when training the associated PMs (depending on the phase of the project). However, we also deployed models trained on different mixtures of helpfulness and harmlessness data.
items.zh: 基于 RLHF 微调的模型：我们在界面中使用了一系列这样的模型。这些模型主要根据训练相关 PM 时可用的数据量而有所不同（取决于项目阶段）。然而，我们也部署了在有用性和无害性数据不同混合上训练的模型。

items.en: In the final phase of the project, when we were primarily deploying RLHF-finetuned models, we often deployed several such models at once. This allowed us to monitor progress by gathering model-comparison data, and also to (perhaps) improve data diversity.
items.zh: 在项目的最后阶段，当我们主要部署基于 RLHF 微调的模型时，我们经常同时部署多个这样的模型。这使我们能够通过收集模型比较数据来监控进展，并且（可能）提高数据多样性。

items.en: Corresponding to the three classes of models, we divide our data into three distributions:
items.zh: 对应于三类模型，我们将数据分为三个分布：

items.en: A core base dataset collected using only the context-distilled LM. This dataset includes 44k helpfulness comparisons and 42k red-teaming (harmlessness) comparisons (note a conversation typically comprises about four comparisons).
items.zh: 一个核心基础数据集，仅使用上下文蒸馏 LM 收集。该数据集包含 44k 个有用性比较和 42k 个红队测试（无害性）比较（注意一个对话通常包含约四个比较）。

items.en: A RS dataset consisting of 52k helpfulness comparisons and 2k red-teaming comparison using rejection sampling models, where rejection sampling used a preference model trained on the base dataset.
items.zh: 一个 RS 数据集，包含 52k 个有用性比较和 2k 个红队测试比较，使用拒绝采样模型，其中拒绝采样使用了在基础数据集上训练的偏好模型。

items.en: An iterated ‘online’ dataset including data from RLHF models, which were updated on a roughly weekly cadence over the course of about five weeks. This dataset contains 22k helpfulness comparisons and no red-teaming data.
items.zh: 一个迭代的“在线”数据集，包含来自 RLHF 模型的数据，这些模型在大约五周内以大致每周一次的节奏更新。该数据集包含 22k 个有用性比较，没有红队测试数据。

items.en: A histogram of these distributions by our final, online HH preference model’s score can be seen in Figure 15 in Section 4.5. In what follows when we discuss the static or base+RS dataset, we will be referring to the combination of the first two components. Our ‘online’ RLHF models in Section 4.5 are trained on all three components. Most of our results are based on the static dataset, as we conducted experiments and evaluations with it while the online data collection was underway.
items.zh: 这些分布按我们最终的在线 HH 偏好模型得分的直方图可见第 4.5 节的图 15。在后续讨论中，当我们提到静态或基础+RS 数据集时，指的是前两个部分的组合。第 4.5 节中的“在线”RLHF 模型是在所有三个部分上训练的。我们的大部分结果基于静态数据集，因为我们在在线数据收集进行期间使用它进行了实验和评估。

items.en: We analyze a few different splits of the static dataset – a standard split into 95/5 train/test data, and a 65/35 split that we use in order to obtain better statistics when evaluating preference model calibration on the test set. We also consider a 50/50 split, where we train distinct PMs on the two halves of the dataset. This is used to evaluate the robustness of RL training, as we then train an RL policy against one PM while evaluating the rewards achieved by that policy as measured by the independent PM.
items.zh: 我们分析了静态数据集的几个不同划分——标准的 95/5 训练/测试数据划分，以及我们使用的 65/35 划分，以便在测试集上评估偏好模型校准度时获得更好的统计量。我们还考虑了一个 50/50 划分，我们在数据集的两半上训练不同的 PM。这用于评估 RL 训练的鲁棒性，因为我们随后针对一个 PM 训练 RL 策略，同时评估该策略由独立 PM 测量的奖励。

items.en: A significant part of our analysis compares models against each other in order to generate associated Elo scores, as described in [Askell et al., 2021]. That is, we have crowdworkers chat with two models simultaneously, with each model generating one response (either ‘A’ or ‘B’) at each turn, and we record the sample that is preferred by the worker. This provides us with a record of ‘win rates’ between pairs of models, which we can then fit to corresponding Elo scores, to produce Figure 1 (where we show both win rates and Elo scores). Two useful conversion formulas are
items.zh: 我们分析的一个重要部分是将模型相互比较，以生成相关的 Elo 分数，如 [Askell et al., 2021] 所述。也就是说，我们让众包工作者同时与两个模型聊天，每个模型在每一轮生成一个回答（“A”或“B”），并记录工作者偏好的样本。这为我们提供了模型对之间的“胜率”记录，然后我们可以将其拟合到相应的 Elo 分数，以生成图 1（其中我们同时显示了胜率和 Elo 分数）。两个有用的转换公式是

items.en: for the fraction in which one model is preferred over another, the difference in Elo scores, and our PM scores. Note that conceptually win fractions, Elo scores and PM scores are interchangeable; we keep both Elo and PM scores so that we can avoid confusing crowdworker preferences (where we use Elo) with our preference modeling and RLHF (where we use PM scores).
items.zh: 对于一种模型优于另一种模型的分数、Elo分数差异以及我们的PM分数。请注意，从概念上讲，胜率、Elo分数和PM分数是可互换的；我们同时保留Elo和PM分数，以便避免混淆众包工人的偏好（我们使用Elo）与我们的偏好建模和基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）（我们使用PM分数）。

items.capEn: Figure 7: (left) We show the learning curves for PM accuracy when training on a mixture of the static helpfulness and harmlessness (i.e, ‘HH’) data distributions. Since we train for one epoch, these results also give sense for dataset-size scaling of accuracy. (right) We show the model size dependence of HH static preference model accuracy.
items.capZh: 图7：（左）我们展示了在静态有用性和无害性（即'HH'）数据分布的混合上训练时PM准确率的学习曲线。由于我们只训练一个周期，这些结果也给出了准确率随数据集规模缩放的感觉。（右）我们展示了HH静态偏好模型准确率对模型大小的依赖性。

items.en: Note that the Elo scores for context-distilled models in Figure 1 differ somewhat from the analogous results for prompted models in [Askell et al., 2021] – the Elo scores are now more compressed. The main difference is that we did not use top-p sampling this time121212We found that our RLHF models gave more preferable responses without top-p sampling, presumably because that’s how they were trained, so we decided to remove top-p sampling when comparing snapshot Elos, including the context-distilled models which are the initial snapshots of all RLHF models.. The difference may also be due to changes in the crowdworker distribution since that earlier experiment, or changes in crowdworker expectations, as before this test our workers were mostly interacting with higher-quality RLHF-trained models.
items.zh: 请注意，图1中上下文蒸馏模型的Elo分数与[Askell et al., 2021]中提示模型的结果有所不同——Elo分数现在更加压缩。主要区别在于这次我们没有使用top-p采样121212我们发现我们的RLHF模型在没有top-p采样的情况下给出了更受欢迎的响应，大概是因为它们就是这样训练的，因此我们决定在比较快照Elo时移除top-p采样，包括作为所有RLHF模型初始快照的上下文蒸馏模型。。这种差异也可能是由于自那项早期实验以来众包工人分布的变化，或众包工人期望的变化，因为在此测试之前，我们的工人主要与更高质量的RLHF训练模型互动。

items.en: We use language models with specifications that are identical to those discussed in [Askell et al., 2021], with a total of seven language models with parameter counts running from 13M to 52B and approximating a geometric series with increments of roughly 4×4\times. We use PyTorch [Paszke et al., 2019] and Triton [Tillet et al., 2019] to facilitate model training and performance. Our preference model training setup is also identical to that in [Askell et al., 2021], and in particular we apply ‘preference model pretraining’ (PMP) to our language models before finetuning on our human feedback datasets, as explained in Section 4 of that paper. More details are provided in Appendix A. Note that we typically only train PMs for a single epoch, so the learning curves themselves (Figure 7 left) indicate how performance scales with dataset size (we used a fixed learning rate).
items.zh: 我们使用的语言模型规格与[Askell et al., 2021]中讨论的完全相同，共有七个语言模型，参数数量从13M到52B，近似几何级数，增量约为4×4\times。我们使用PyTorch [Paszke et al., 2019]和Triton [Tillet et al., 2019]来促进模型训练和性能。我们的偏好模型训练设置也与[Askell et al., 2021]中的相同，特别是我们在人类反馈数据集上微调之前，对语言模型应用了'偏好模型预训练'（PMP），如该论文第4节所述。更多细节见附录A。请注意，我们通常只训练PM一个周期，因此学习曲线本身（图7左）指示了性能如何随数据集规模缩放（我们使用了固定学习率）。

items.en: We would like to understand how preference modeling performance improves as we increase model size and collect additional data. In Figure 7 we show basic results for PM accuracy when training on our static helpful and harmless data mixture. Roughly speaking, we observe log-linear trends in both dataset and model size. We tend to find somewhat more consistent trends if we model only the helpfulness or harmlessness distributions in isolation, rather than as a mixture, as observed in Figure 32 in Appendix A.3. But there we also see that for some data distributions [Stiennon et al., 2020], scaling trends can exhibit more complex patterns that defy simple trends.
items.zh: 我们想了解偏好建模性能如何随着模型大小的增加和额外数据的收集而提高。在图7中，我们展示了在静态有用和无害数据混合上训练时PM准确率的基本结果。粗略地说，我们观察到在数据集和模型大小方面都存在对数线性趋势。如果我们单独建模有用性或无害性分布而不是混合分布，我们往往会发现更一致的趋势，如附录A.3中的图32所示。但在那里我们也看到，对于某些数据分布[Stiennon et al., 2020]，缩放趋势可能表现出更复杂的模式，违背简单的趋势。

items.en: Our preference modeling data comes from natural language dialogue, where crowdworkers have text-based conversations with the model, and choose the more helpful of two model responses at every turn in the conversation (or the more harmful one, for red-teaming tasks). So it is natural to ask how PM performance changes as a function of the conversational turn. We show these results in Figure 8. PMs are somewhat more accurate on the first step of the conversation, but their accuracy is nearly constant thereafter.
items.zh: 我们的偏好建模数据来自自然语言对话，其中众包工人与模型进行基于文本的对话，并在对话的每一轮选择两个模型响应中更有帮助的一个（或更有害的一个，用于红队任务）。因此，很自然地要问PM性能如何随对话轮次变化。我们在图8中展示了这些结果。PM在对话的第一步稍微更准确，但此后准确率几乎恒定。

items.capEn: Figure 8: (left) Distribution of conversational turns in a large held-out test set used to investigate calibration and accuracy. (right) We examine preference model accuracy as a function of the number of exchanges in the conversation.
items.capZh: 图8：（左）用于研究校准和准确率的大型保留测试集中对话轮次的分布。（右）我们检查偏好模型准确率作为对话中交换次数的函数。

full.sec: 3.3 Calibration of Preference Models and Implications for RL
full.secZh: 3.3 偏好模型的校准及其对强化学习的影响

items.en: Preference model scores should predict the probability that humans will prefer one or another model-generated response. We are interested in whether these probabilities are accurate, i.e. whether the PMs are well calibrated. We characterize calibration in Figure 9, where we display PM accuracy as a function of the difference in PM scores assigned to pairs of samples, along with a heavy black line representing perfect calibration. We observe that PMs trained only on helpfulness data are very well calibrated, but PMs trained on a mixture of helpful and harmless data are slightly under-confident.
items.zh: 偏好模型分数应预测人类更偏好一个模型生成的响应而非另一个的概率。我们关心这些概率是否准确，即PM是否校准良好。我们在图9中描述了校准情况，其中我们展示了PM准确率作为分配给样本对的PM分数差值的函数，并有一条粗黑线表示完美校准。我们观察到，仅在有帮助性数据上训练的PM校准非常好，但在有帮助和无害数据混合上训练的PM稍微不够自信。

items.en: These calibration results are important because in later sections we will be using PM scores as the reward signal for reinforcement learning. Since the PM scores are well-calibrated, we can trust that they faithfully encode the probabilities that humans will prefer specific model samples (at least on-distribution with the training set). This means that when we see RL robustly achieving a given reward, we can trust that those who interact with this model (if they are well-represented by our crowdworker distribution) will prefer it to reference models at a predictable rate, provided that the PM scores of the models’ responses are within the range considered in these calibration studies. That said, we find significant failures of robustness as RLHF optimizes towards much higher scores, as explained in Section 4.5 and Appendix B.4.
items.zh: 这些校准结果很重要，因为在后面的章节中，我们将使用PM分数作为强化学习的奖励信号。由于PM分数校准良好，我们可以相信它们忠实地编码了人类偏好特定模型样本的概率（至少在训练集分布上）。这意味着当我们看到RL稳健地达到给定奖励时，我们可以相信与这个模型互动的人（如果他们能很好地代表我们的众包工人分布）会以可预测的速率偏好它而非参考模型，前提是模型响应的PM分数在这些校准研究考虑的范围内。也就是说，我们发现当RLHF优化到更高分数时，存在显著的鲁棒性失败，如第4.5节和附录B.4所述。

items.en: We might generally expect that as sample quality improves, it becomes more difficult to reliably identify the very best samples. In Figure 25 in the appendix, we show that if we restrict to comparisons where both samples have a PM score above a given threshold, then PM accuracy drops as a function of this threshold value (to compare different PMs we add an offset so that they all obtain the same mean score on the test distribution). Notice that this result combines three effects: (1) differentiation among more sophisticated samples may be more challenging and require greater capabilities, (2) we have fewer very high quality samples to learn from in our dataset, as they are non-generic, and (3) pairs of samples that are all high-quality will have similar scores (compared to randomly chosen pairs), and so be more difficult to distinguish.
items.zh: 我们通常可以预期，随着样本质量的提高，可靠地识别最佳样本变得更加困难。在附录的图25中，我们表明，如果限制在两个样本的PM分数都高于给定阈值的比较中，那么PM准确率会随着该阈值值的增加而下降（为了比较不同的PM，我们添加一个偏移量，使它们在测试分布上获得相同的平均分数）。请注意，这个结果结合了三个效应：（1）区分更复杂的样本可能更具挑战性，需要更强的能力；（2）我们的数据集中高质量样本较少，因为它们不常见；（3）所有高质量的样本对将具有相似的分数（与随机选择的样本对相比），因此更难区分。

items.en: These observations also have an implication for RLHF training, namely that we should expect diminishing returns from further RLHF training once our policies achieve a sufficiently high PM score. This also motivates online training, so that we can update our PMs to stay on-distribution as RLHF policies improve.
items.zh: 这些观察结果对RLHF训练也有启示，即一旦我们的策略达到足够高的PM分数，我们应该预期进一步RLHF训练的收益递减。这也激发了在线训练，以便我们可以在RLHF策略改进时更新我们的PM以保持分布内。

items.capEn: Figure 9: We show preference modeling accuracy as a function of the difference in PM score between higher and lower ranked responses. The black lines indicate the calibrated prediction of accuracy 1/(1+e−Δ)11superscript𝑒Δ1/(1+e^{-\Delta}), where ΔΔ\Delta is the score difference. On the (left) we show calibration for a PM trained and evaluated on all our static data, while on the (right) we show results for a model trained and evaluated only on our helpful data distribution. We see that calibration is slightly worse for models trained on the HH mixture.
items.capZh: 图9：我们展示了偏好建模准确率作为较高和较低排名响应之间PM分数差值的函数。黑线表示校准预测的准确率1/(1+e−Δ)11superscript𝑒Δ1/(1+e^{-\Delta})，其中Δ\Delta是分数差值。（左）我们展示了在全部静态数据上训练和评估的PM的校准情况，（右）我们展示了仅在有用性数据分布上训练和评估的模型的结果。我们看到，在HH混合上训练的模型校准稍差。

full.sec: 3.4 Evaluating Helpful and Harmless Preference Models
full.secZh: 3.4 评估有用与无害偏好模型

items.capEn: Figure 10: (left) We show a histogram of the 52B static PM predictions for the HHH evaluations. The three confidently incorrect outliers all contrast responses where the model declares its ignorance instead of providing a thorough and sophisticated-sounding response that contains misleading information. So they are failures of honesty and accuracy. (right) Here we show agreement rates between Anthropic researchers, our crowdworkers, and a static preference model on 320 samples from the static test set.
items.capZh: 图10：（左）我们展示了52B静态PM对HHH评估预测的直方图。三个自信错误的异常值都对比了模型声明无知而不是提供全面且听起来复杂的响应（包含误导信息）的响应。因此，它们是诚实性和准确性的失败。（右）我们展示了Anthropic研究人员、我们的众包工人和静态偏好模型在静态测试集的320个样本上的一致率。

items.en: We have shown that PMs trained on our dataset are learning something and are well-calibrated. But we would also like to show that these PMs are actually learning to prefer helpful and harmless behavior, according to some independent standard. We provided a set of comparison evaluations for helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness (i.e., HHH Evaluation) in prior work and evaluated raw, context-distilled, and prompted language models [Askell et al., 2021] on this benchmark. As shown in Figure 5, we find that our PMs perform much better than all of the models we evaluated previously. In fact, the Pathways LM effort [Chowdhery et al., 2022] recently reported a mean human score of 75% on this dataset, so in a sense our PM performance of 86% is well above mean human accuracy.
items.zh: 我们已经表明，在我们的数据集上训练的PM正在学习某些东西并且校准良好。但我们也想表明，根据某些独立标准，这些PM实际上正在学习偏好有帮助和无害的行为。我们在先前的工作中提供了一组用于有帮助性、诚实性和无害性（即HHH评估）的比较评估，并在此基准上评估了原始、上下文蒸馏和提示语言模型[Askell et al., 2021]。如图5所示，我们发现我们的PM表现优于我们之前评估的所有模型。事实上，Pathways LM团队[Chowdhery et al., 2022]最近报告了该数据集上75%的平均人类分数，因此从某种意义上说，我们86%的PM性能远高于平均人类准确率。

items.en: We also observe that there are a few outliers, where our PMs are confidently incorrect on HHH evaluation comparisons (see Figure 10 left). These are all examples of failures of honesty, where the better choice is honest but unhelpful, while the incorrect choice appears both helpful and well-written, but is subtly incorrect or misleading. We show the single worst example in Figure 11. It’s worth noting that the bad response (which was written by Anthropic researchers) is likely out of distribution as compared to samples from our language models and RL policies. Since only model-generated samples were used to train the PMs, this may explain why the PM was badly fooled by these examples. It also indicates that the PMs are not adversarially robust.
items.zh: 我们还观察到，有一些异常值，我们的PM在HHH评估比较中自信地错误（见图10左）。这些都是诚实性失败的例子，其中更好的选择是诚实但无帮助的，而错误的选择看起来既有帮助又写得很好，但微妙地不正确或具有误导性。我们在图11中展示了最糟糕的一个例子。值得注意的是，糟糕的响应（由Anthropic研究人员编写）可能相对于我们语言模型和RL策略的样本是分布外的。由于只有模型生成的样本用于训练PM，这可能解释了为什么PM被这些例子严重欺骗。这也表明PM不具有对抗鲁棒性。

items.en: We can also compare our crowdworkers, ourselves, and our preference models in terms of agreement on labels. The results are shown on the right in Figure 10, and are based on about 320 examples from our static test set. The PMs are trained to predict crowdworker behavior, so PM-Crowdworker agreement is best. However, the largest PM actually agrees with the authors (i.e. Anthropic researchers) slightly more than the authors agree with crowdworkers on labels. We also suspect this is a poor subsample of the data, since PM accuracy is several percent lower on this subsample as compared to the full test set.
items.zh: 我们还可以比较我们的众包工人、我们自己以及我们的偏好模型在标签上的一致性。结果如图10右侧所示，基于静态测试集中约320个示例。PM被训练来预测众包工人的行为，因此PM-众包工人一致性最好。然而，最大的PM实际上与作者（即Anthropic研究人员）的一致性略高于作者与众包工人在标签上的一致性。我们也怀疑这是数据的一个糟糕子样本，因为该子样本上的PM准确率比整个测试集低几个百分点。

items.en: We can begin to evaluate whether our PMs display gender bias by comparing scores across gendered terms. To this purpose we evaluate preference model scores on short dialogues of the form
items.zh: 我们可以通过比较不同性别术语的分数来开始评估我们的PM是否表现出性别偏见。为此，我们评估了以下形式的短对话上的偏好模型分数

items.en: where we use a list of 76 occupations, 12 matched gender terms (mother vs father, guy vs gal, etc), and both present and past tense [Rae et al., 2021]. Preference model scores are not directly meaningful, so instead we evaluate the difference in score between paired gender terms, and compare that to the difference in score among terms within a given gender. The results are shown on the left in Figure 12. We observe that the variation among terms that indicate a given gender appears to be at least as large as the variation across genders. So it would appear that the PMs do not exhibit a substantial bias.
items.zh: 其中我们使用了76个职业列表、12个匹配的性别术语（母亲与父亲、男人与女人等），以及现在时和过去时[Rae et al., 2021]。偏好模型分数本身没有直接意义，因此我们评估配对性别术语之间的分数差异，并将其与给定性别内术语之间的分数差异进行比较。结果如图12左侧所示。我们观察到，表示给定性别的术语之间的变异似乎至少与跨性别的变异一样大。因此，PM似乎没有表现出显著的偏见。

items.capEn: Figure 12: These plots show score or score-difference distributions from the static preference models on alignment-relevant data distributions. (left) We evaluate Bot Adversarial Dialogues [Xu et al., 2020] that have been designated as harmful or not. (right) We show distributions of preference model score differences for statements of the form ‘The CEO was a lady’, comparing differences among words within a gender category (e.g. gentleman vs boyfriend) and across genders (e.g. mother vs father, lady vs gentleman) while holding occupations fixed.
items.capZh: 图12：这些图显示了静态偏好模型在与对齐相关的数据分布上的分数或分数差异分布。（左）我们评估了被标记为有害或无害的机器人对抗对话[Xu et al., 2020]。（右）我们展示了对于形式为'The CEO was a lady'的陈述，偏好模型分数差异的分布，比较了性别类别内词语（例如gentleman与boyfriend）和跨性别词语（例如mother与father, lady与gentleman）之间的差异，同时保持职业固定。

items.en: As an external validation of our PMs, we consider the Bot Adversarial Dialogues (BAD) dataset [Xu et al., 2020]. This dataset contains a few thousand conversations between an AI system and a human. Each AI response is labeled as harmful or not harmful. We compute the distribution of preference model scores for BAD AI utterances (we restrict our analysis to the first BAD AI utterance per conversation) and find that the BAD AI utterances marked as harmful have significantly lower preference model scores. This suggests that our PMs are effectively classifying these AI generated utterances, even though they are likely quite different from the data distribution that our PMs were trained on.
items.zh: 作为我们PM的外部验证，我们考虑了机器人对抗对话（BAD）数据集[Xu et al., 2020]。该数据集包含AI系统与人类之间的几千次对话。每个AI响应被标记为有害或无害。我们计算了BAD AI话语的偏好模型分数分布（我们将分析限制在每次对话的第一个BAD AI话语），发现被标记为有害的BAD AI话语具有显著较低的偏好模型分数。这表明我们的PM有效地分类了这些AI生成的话语，尽管它们很可能与我们PM训练的数据分布非常不同。

items.en: We apply reinforcement learning (RL) with preference modeling, following the approach outlined in [Stiennon et al., 2020], which can summarized in the following steps:
items.zh: 我们采用带有偏好建模的强化学习（RL），遵循[Stiennon et al., 2020]中概述的方法，该方法可总结为以下步骤：

items.en: Prepare a dataset of comparisons, and train a PM to assign a higher score to the ‘better’ item in each comparison. In the context of our human feedback experiments, each comparison consists of a prompt followed by a pair of model-generated responses, with a PM score evaluated at the end of each response.
items.zh: 准备一个比较数据集，并训练一个偏好模型（PM），使其在每次比较中为“更好”的项分配更高的分数。在我们的基于人类反馈的实验中，每次比较包括一个提示和一对模型生成的回复，并在每个回复结束时评估PM分数。

items.en: Extract all the prompts from the preceding dataset, and train an RL policy to generate a response to each prompt autoregressively, with a reward signal provided by the PM score at the end of the response.
items.zh: 从上述数据集中提取所有提示，并训练一个RL策略，使其自回归地生成每个提示的回复，并在回复结束时由PM分数提供奖励信号。

items.en: PM dataset and training details are provided in Appendix A.2; we also discussed the performance of our PMs in Section 3. In the language of RL, each response generated by the policy is a ‘timestep’, a full conversation is one ‘trajectory’, and the PM score is a single ‘reward’ provided at the end.
items.zh: PM数据集和训练细节见附录A.2；我们还在第3节讨论了PM的性能。在RL术语中，策略生成的每个回复是一个“时间步”，完整的对话是一个“轨迹”，而PM分数是在结束时提供的单一“奖励”。

items.en: The idea is to use the preference model to steer the policy towards writing better responses. However, as we saw in earlier sections, PMs also become less calibrated at higher scores, so higher rewards do not necessarily imply better performance.
items.zh: 其思想是利用偏好模型引导策略生成更好的回复。然而，正如我们在前面章节中看到的，PM在较高分数下校准性变差，因此更高的奖励并不一定意味着更好的性能。

items.en: To stabilize RL training, we use Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) [Schulman et al., 2017]. We also follow other work [Stiennon et al., 2020] and apply an empirically-estimated KL penalty term in the reward, with the total reward given by
items.zh: 为了稳定RL训练，我们使用近端策略优化（PPO）[Schulman et al., 2017]。我们还遵循其他工作[Stiennon et al., 2020]，在奖励中应用经验估计的KL惩罚项，总奖励由下式给出：

items.en: where λKL≥0subscript𝜆KL0\lambda_{\rm KL}\geq 0 is a hyperparameter. In practice we use a very small value of λKL=0.001subscript𝜆KL0.001\lambda_{\rm KL}=0.001, which likely has a very minor impact during most of RL training (as DKL<100subscript𝐷KL100D_{\rm KL}<100 typically), and might actually be wholly unnecessary. More details about RL are provided in B.1.
items.zh: 其中λKL≥0是一个超参数。实践中我们使用非常小的λKL=0.001，这在RL训练的大部分时间中影响很小（因为DKL通常<100），实际上可能完全不需要。更多RL细节见B.1。

items.en: Throughout this paper we use rPM=subscript𝑟PMabsentr_{\rm PM}= the preference model score itself for the RL reward. Recall that as implied by equation (2.1), this means that the difference in rPMsubscript𝑟PMr_{\rm PM} values between two samples A𝐴A and B𝐵B will be related to the predicted probability P​(A>B)𝑃𝐴𝐵P(A>B) that A𝐴A will be preferred to B𝐵B via
items.zh: 在本文中，我们使用rPM = 偏好模型分数本身作为RL奖励。回想一下，如公式(2.1)所示，这意味着两个样本A和B之间rPM的差值将与预测概率P(A>B)（即A优于B的概率）相关，通过以下方式：

items.en: There is no good reason131313For example we expect it may be better to penalize bad behavior more strongly to attempt to improve worst-case model outputs. to use this preference model score directly as the reward, but it has been used in prior work such as [Stiennon et al., 2020] and so for simplicity we will not explore variations on this choice here.
items.zh: 没有充分的理由直接使用这个偏好模型分数作为奖励，但它在先前的工作如[Stiennon et al., 2020]中被使用，因此为简单起见，我们在此不探索其他选择。

items.en: In order to produce additional prompts (i.e. the human side of the conversations) for RLHF training, we used a large LM to generate them. For this purpose, we simply used few-shot learning, creating a context with about 10 existing high-quality human queries, and then sampling to generate more. We find that the sample efficiency of RLHF is roughly the same on the original crowdworker-written prompt dataset and the model-generated one, so we combine the two for greater diversity during RLHF training. We used 137k prompts from the ‘static’ dataset, and 369k model-generated prompts.
items.zh: 为了生成用于RLHF训练的额外提示（即对话中的人类方），我们使用了一个大型语言模型来生成它们。为此，我们简单地使用少样本学习，创建一个包含约10个现有高质量人类查询的上下文，然后采样生成更多。我们发现RLHF在原始众包工人编写的提示数据集和模型生成的提示数据集上的样本效率大致相同，因此我们将两者结合以增加RLHF训练期间的多样性。我们使用了来自“静态”数据集的13.7万个提示和36.9万个模型生成的提示。

items.en: Note that almost all of our preference modeling data was collected from 52B models. This means that RLHF training with smaller models might have been challenging, since samples from smaller models tend to be out-of-distribution from the PM training data. Thus it is quite interesting that models more than fifty times smaller were actually able to learn and improve, as seen in Figure 1.
items.zh: 请注意，我们几乎所有的偏好建模数据都是从52B模型收集的。这意味着使用较小模型进行RLHF训练可能具有挑战性，因为较小模型的样本往往偏离PM训练数据的分布。因此，有趣的是，如图1所示，小五十倍以上的模型实际上能够学习和改进。

items.en: We now discuss the problem of RLHF robustness. A fully robust PM would agree with humans on distributions of dialogues quite different from those encountered during PM training (i.e. different from those created by crowdworker interactions with our deployed AI assistants). However, we do not expect that our PMs are so robust, and in fact Figure 11 provides one plausible example of a robustness failure. Since RL optimizes the policy to maximize the PM score, any failure in robustness on the part of the PM may be exploited by the RL policy to achieve higher rewards, without actually improving the policy’s behavior from the point of view of human evaluators.
items.zh: 我们现在讨论RLHF的鲁棒性问题。一个完全鲁棒的PM会在与PM训练期间遇到的对话分布（即众包工人与我们部署的AI助手交互产生的分布）相当不同的对话分布上与人类保持一致。然而，我们不期望我们的PM如此鲁棒，实际上图11提供了一个鲁棒性失败的合理例子。由于RL优化策略以最大化PM分数，PM方面的任何鲁棒性失败都可能被RL策略利用以获得更高的奖励，而实际上并未从人类评估者的角度改进策略的行为。

items.en: A rigorous way to study robustness is to take snapshots of the policy at various points during RLHF training, including the initial snapshot, and have crowdworkers compare their performance. This gives a ‘true’ Elo score, as evaluated by crowdworkers, which can then be compared directly with the PM scores. We present an example of this study in Section 4.5.
items.zh: 研究鲁棒性的一种严格方法是在RLHF训练的不同时间点（包括初始时间点）获取策略的快照，并让众包工人比较它们的性能。这给出了一个由众包工人评估的“真实”Elo分数，然后可以直接与PM分数进行比较。我们在第4.5节给出了这样一个研究的例子。

items.en: However, this sort of test requires collecting additional human feedback data, which can be slow and expensive, so here we also study robustness from a different angle. Similar to how datasets are split into train and test sets for supervised learning, we split our preference model comparison data into two halves (a train half and a test half), and train separate preference models on each, which we refer to as the train PM’s and the test PM’s. We then train RLHF policies against the train PM’s, while evaluating them using the test PM’s. Similar to how test set evaluations help us understand overfitting in supervised learning, test PM evaluations help us understand overfitting relative to the train PM’s. These experiments are not conclusive since the train and test PMs may exhibit correlated robustness failures.
items.zh: 然而，这种测试需要收集额外的人类反馈数据，这可能既慢又昂贵，因此我们在这里也从不同角度研究鲁棒性。类似于监督学习中将数据集分为训练集和测试集，我们将偏好模型比较数据分成两半（训练一半和测试一半），并在每一半上训练单独的偏好模型，分别称为训练PM和测试PM。然后我们针对训练PM训练RLHF策略，同时使用测试PM评估它们。类似于测试集评估帮助我们理解监督学习中的过拟合，测试PM评估帮助我们理解相对于训练PM的过拟合。这些实验并非结论性的，因为训练PM和测试PM可能表现出相关的鲁棒性失败。

items.en: The main conclusions from these experiments are: (1) RLHF becomes gradually less robust at higher PM scores, and (2) larger preference models are more robust than smaller ones.
items.zh: 这些实验的主要结论是：（1）RLHF在较高PM分数下逐渐变得不那么鲁棒，（2）较大的偏好模型比较小的更鲁棒。

items.en: We conduct two sets of experiments as follows:
items.zh: 我们进行两组实验如下：

items.en: Train PM Size = 52B: This set consists of a scan of policies (i.e. one for each model size), all of which are trained with respect to the same 52B train PM.
items.zh: 训练PM大小 = 52B：这组包括一系列策略（即每个模型大小一个），所有策略都针对同一个52B训练PM进行训练。

items.en: Train PM Size = Policy Size: This set consists of a scan of policies, with each policy trained with respect to a train PM of the same size as the policy.
items.zh: 训练PM大小 = 策略大小：这组包括一系列策略，每个策略针对与其大小相同的训练PM进行训练。

items.en: For both experiments, each policy is further evaluated with respected to a scan of test PM’s throughout training. Note that a scan refers to 7 different model sizes ranging from 13M to 52B, thus giving us 7 policies and 7×7777\times 7 evaluations per experiment.
items.zh: 对于两个实验，每个策略进一步在整个训练过程中使用一系列测试PM进行评估。注意，一系列指的是7种不同的模型大小，从13M到52B，因此每个实验有7个策略和7×7次评估。

items.en: In Figure 4, we compare the train PM and test PM scores throughout the training process, similar to how train and test curves are often compared for supervised training. We find that in all cases, the two scores are in close agreement during early stages of training, but eventually diverge, with the test PM providing a lower score. The divergence is likely an indication that the preference model is less robust and more easily exploited at higher rewards. That is, the policy has been over-optimized on the train PM, making the train PM over-confident in the policy’s performance. The test PM, on the other hand, doesn’t suffer from this problem since it was trained on a different portion of data that neither the policy nor the train PM had observed.
items.zh: 在图4中，我们比较了训练过程中训练PM和测试PM的分数，类似于监督训练中通常比较训练曲线和测试曲线。我们发现，在所有情况下，两个分数在训练早期阶段非常接近，但最终出现分歧，测试PM给出较低的分数。这种分歧可能表明偏好模型在较高奖励下鲁棒性较差且更容易被利用。也就是说，策略在训练PM上被过度优化，使得训练PM对策略的性能过于自信。另一方面，测试PM没有这个问题，因为它是在不同的数据部分上训练的，策略和训练PM都没有观察到这些数据。

items.en: We provide more discussion in Appendix B.2.
items.zh: 我们在附录B.2中提供更多讨论。

items.capEn: Figure 13: These figures show training curves in the KLKL\sqrt{\mathrm{KL}} vs PM score plane, exhibiting the approximate linear relationship between these variables, especially in the left-hand plot using the more highly-performing 52B PMs. We observe some instability in the smaller models, likely because the training data for all our PMs was created with 52B language models, and the much smaller LM samples tend to be quite OOD for the PMs. Finally, by comparing the left and right-hand plots, we see that training against smaller PMs (matched to policy sizes) eventually results in poor performance, as evaluated by the 52B PM. Some of our runs were cut off early as they became unstable. We found that smaller models were generally more difficult to stabilize.
items.capZh: 图13：这些图展示了在KL√\sqrt{\mathrm{KL}} vs PM得分平面上的训练曲线，显示出这些变量之间近似线性关系，尤其是在使用性能更高的52B PM的左图中。我们观察到较小模型存在一些不稳定性，可能是因为我们所有PM的训练数据都是由52B语言模型创建的，而小得多的LM样本对于PM来说往往是相当分布外的。最后，通过比较左右图，我们看到针对较小PM（与策略大小匹配）进行训练最终会导致性能较差，如52B PM所评估的那样。我们的一些运行因变得不稳定而提前终止。我们发现较小的模型通常更难稳定。

full.sec: 4.3 An Approximately Linear Relation Between DKLsubscript𝐷KL\sqrt{D_{\rm KL}} and Reward
full.secZh: 4.3 DKL与奖励的近似线性关系

items.en: In Figures 4 and 13 we observe an approximately linear relation between KLKL\sqrt{\mathrm{KL}} and PM score during RLHF training. Furthermore, we note that when all models are trained and evaluated with the same PMs, the learning curves are roughly parallel in the DKLsubscript𝐷KL\sqrt{D_{\rm KL}}-reward plane. Note that here the ‘KL’ is more precisely DKL(π||π0)D_{\rm KL}(\pi||\pi_{0}), where π𝜋\pi denotes the policy distribution (and π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} the initial policy), as evaluated empirically on the samples drawn from the policy during training.
items.zh: 在图4和图13中，我们观察到在RLHF训练期间KL√\sqrt{\mathrm{KL}}与PM得分之间存在近似线性关系。此外，我们注意到当所有模型使用相同的PM进行训练和评估时，学习曲线在DKL\sqrt{D_{\rm KL}}-奖励平面中大致平行。注意，这里的'KL'更精确地说是DKL(π||π0)D_{\rm KL}(\pi||\pi_{0})，其中π\pi表示策略分布（π0\pi_{0}为初始策略），根据训练期间从策略中抽取的样本进行经验评估。

items.en: Why should this be? When DKL(π+δπ||π)D_{\rm KL}(\pi+\delta\pi||\pi) is series expanded in δ​π𝛿𝜋\delta\pi, the expansion begins at quadratic order, so if we imagine that the RL policy can also be series expanded around the base LM, and that the RL reward varies linearly in δ​π𝛿𝜋\delta\pi, then in the ‘small-δ​π𝛿𝜋\delta\pi region’ (i.e. where the series expansion provides a good approximation), we should expect reward ∝DKLproportional-toabsentsubscript𝐷KL\propto\sqrt{D_{\rm KL}}. Typically we should expect that reward varies linearly in δ​π𝛿𝜋\delta\pi, because because the initial policy π𝜋\pi was not previously optimized for reward, so there is no reason why it would sit at an extremum with respect to small variations δ​π𝛿𝜋\delta\pi. So the fact that this relation seems to hold empirically suggests that most of RLHF training remains in the small-δ​π𝛿𝜋\delta\pi regime.
items.zh: 为什么会这样？当DKL(π+δπ||π)D_{\rm KL}(\pi+\delta\pi||\pi)对δπ\delta\pi进行级数展开时，展开从二次项开始，因此如果我们假设RL策略也可以围绕基础LM进行级数展开，并且RL奖励在δπ\delta\pi中线性变化，那么在'小δπ\delta\pi区域'（即级数展开提供良好近似的区域），我们应该预期奖励∝DKL\propto\sqrt{D_{\rm KL}}。通常我们应该预期奖励在δπ\delta\pi中线性变化，因为初始策略π\pi之前并未针对奖励进行优化，所以没有理由认为它处于关于小变化δπ\delta\pi的极值点。因此，这种关系在经验上成立的事实表明，大多数RLHF训练仍处于小δπ\delta\pi区域。

items.en: Though they did not use these coordinates, a similar scaling can be read off from the results in learning to summarize [Stiennon et al., 2020]. In particular, they provide a nice analysis of rejection sampling, where they generate N𝑁N samples, and then plot mean reward of the top k𝑘k samples versus the DKL=log⁡(N/k)subscript𝐷KL𝑁𝑘D_{\rm KL}=\log(N/k). This analysis suggests that these RL learning curves might be associated with changes in the RL policy that behave very similarly to simply rejection sampling from the initial distribution.
items.zh: 尽管他们没有使用这些坐标，但从学习总结的结果[Stiennon et al., 2020]中也可以看出类似的缩放。特别是，他们提供了对拒绝采样的精彩分析，其中他们生成N\N个样本，然后绘制前k\k个样本的平均奖励与DKL=log(N/k)D_{\rm KL}=\log(N/k)的关系图。这一分析表明，这些RL学习曲线可能与RL策略的变化相关联，这些变化的行为与简单地从初始分布中进行拒绝采样非常相似。

items.en: We find this simple relation quite striking, and believe it merits further study. At a conjectural level, it might have a variety of implications and uses when RL-finetuning large generative models:
items.zh: 我们发现这种简单关系相当引人注目，并相信值得进一步研究。在推测层面上，它可能对RL微调大型生成模型具有多种含义和用途：

items.en: These relations provide a rough prediction for ‘how much does the policy need to change to achieve a specific reward’. Furthermore, if the lines corresponding to different model sizes really are parallel, then one can use RL training of a small model along with the zero-shot performance of a larger model to estimate the eventual performance of a larger RL policy. The slopes of these lines also explain how RLHF training can produce such large effective gains in model size, and for example it explains why the RLHF and context-distilled lines in Figure 1 are roughly parallel.
items.zh: 这些关系为'策略需要改变多少才能达到特定奖励'提供了粗略预测。此外，如果不同模型大小对应的线确实是平行的，那么可以利用小模型的RL训练以及大模型的零样本性能来估计更大RL策略的最终性能。这些线的斜率也解释了RLHF训练如何能在模型大小上产生如此大的有效增益，例如，它解释了为什么图1中RLHF和上下文蒸馏线大致平行。

items.en: One can ask a subtle, perhaps ill-defined question about RLHF training – is it teaching the model new skills or simply focusing the model on generating a sub-distribution of existing behaviors. We might attempt to make this distinction sharp by associating the latter class of behaviors with the region where RL reward remains linear in KLKL\sqrt{\rm KL}.
items.zh: 关于RLHF训练，可以提出一个微妙、可能定义不清的问题——它是在教模型新技能，还是仅仅让模型专注于生成现有行为的子分布？我们可以尝试通过将后一类行为与RL奖励保持与KL√\sqrt{\rm KL}线性关系的区域相关联，来使这种区分变得清晰。

items.en: To make some bolder guesses – perhaps the linear relation actually provides an upper bound on RL reward, as a function of the KL. One might also attempt to extend the relation further by replacing KLKL\sqrt{\mathrm{KL}} with a geodesic length in the Fisher geometry.
items.zh: 做出一些更大胆的猜测——也许线性关系实际上提供了RL奖励的上界，作为KL的函数。人们也可以尝试通过将KL√\sqrt{\mathrm{KL}}替换为Fisher几何中的测地线长度来扩展这一关系。

items.en: By making RL learning more predictable and by identifying new quantitative categories of behavior, we might hope to detect unexpected behaviors emerging during RL training.
items.zh: 通过使RL学习更可预测并识别新的定量行为类别，我们可能希望检测到RL训练过程中出现的意外行为。

full.sec: 4.4 Tension Between Helpfulness and Harmlessness in RLHF Training
full.secZh: 4.4 RLHF训练中有用性与无害性之间的张力

items.capEn: Figure 14: (left panel) We show PM score distributions for the helpfulness and red-teaming comparisons using a 52B PMs. (right panel) We train a 52B RLHF policy with respect to the same PM, and periodically evaluate the policy’s performance on held-out prompts (by sampling responses from the policy on such prompts, then evaluating the PM score) from the helpfulness and red-teaming datasets. We find that the policy’s harmlessness score (right, red) is nearly ‘out-of-distribution’ as it’s on the upper tail of the harmless PM data (left, red). On the other hand, the policy’s helpfulness score (right, blue) appears ‘on-distribution’ with respect to the helpfulness PM data (left, blue). In other words, we are over-optimized on harmlessness while we are still likely under-optimized on helpfulness. Dashed lines represent the asymptotic mean of the train scores, to guide the eye in connecting the left and right panels.
items.capZh: 图14：（左图）我们展示了使用52B PM进行有用性和红队比较的PM得分分布。（右图）我们针对同一PM训练了一个52B RLHF策略，并定期评估策略在保留提示上的性能（通过从策略中采样对这些提示的响应，然后评估PM得分），这些提示来自有用性和红队数据集。我们发现策略的无害性得分（右图，红色）几乎处于'分布外'，因为它位于无害PM数据（左图，红色）的上尾。另一方面，策略的有用性得分（右图，蓝色）相对于有用性PM数据（左图，蓝色）看起来是'分布内'的。换句话说，我们在无害性上过度优化，而在有用性上可能仍然优化不足。虚线表示训练得分的渐近均值，以帮助连接左右图。

items.en: Here we discuss a problem we encountered during RLHF training. At an earlier stage of this project, we found that many RLHF policies were very frequently reproducing the same exaggerated responses to all remotely sensitive questions (e.g. recommending users seek therapy and professional help whenever they express any level of displeasure at all). This greatly limited these models’ utility. We still see a vestige of this behavior in some of the examples provided in Section 6.2. We now believe these policies were the result of over-optimizing for harmlessness, while under-optimizing helpfulness.
items.zh: 这里我们讨论在RLHF训练期间遇到的一个问题。在该项目的早期阶段，我们发现许多RLHF策略对所有稍微敏感的问题都非常频繁地重复相同的夸张回应（例如，每当用户表达任何程度的不满时，就建议他们寻求治疗和专业帮助）。这极大地限制了这些模型的实用性。我们在第6.2节提供的一些示例中仍然可以看到这种行为的痕迹。我们现在认为这些策略是过度优化无害性而优化不足有用性的结果。

items.en: With our data collection procedure, we think this is quite intuitive. In order to get a very good score on red-teaming prompts, it’s probably sufficient for models to respond with something like “I can’t answer that.” This does not require much sophistication (it just requires learning to classify harmful requests), and so we expect it is easier to learn than helpfulness.
items.zh: 根据我们的数据收集程序，我们认为这非常直观。为了在红队提示上获得非常好的得分，模型可能只需回应类似“我无法回答这个问题”之类的内容就足够了。这不需要太多复杂性（只需要学习分类有害请求），因此我们预计这比学习有用性更容易。

items.en: In Figure 14 (right), we show the policy’s PM score throughout training, after separating helpfulness and harmlessness prompts. On the left side of the same figure, we show the score distribution of PM comparison data, again separating helpful and harmless datasets. We observe that the policy’s harmlessness score is somewhat off-distribution, as it is on the upper tail of the harmlessness comparison data. On the other hand, the policy’s helpfulness score appears on-distribution, and is likely under-optimized. So we would expect this agent to be very difficult to red-team, but not very helpful.
