items.capEn: Figure 25: These plots show that PM accuracy decreases as we focus exclusively on comparisons between pairs of samples with high score. We have normalized all preference models to have the same mean score on a held-out dataset so that they’re directly comparable, and then plotted accuracy for the comparisons where both samples have scores above a specific threshold.
items.capZh: 图 25：这些图显示，当我们只关注高分样本对之间的比较时，PM 的准确率会下降。我们归一化了所有偏好模型，使它们在保留数据集上的平均得分相同以便直接比较，然后绘制了双方样本得分均高于特定阈值的比较的准确率。

items.capEn: Figure 26: Mean test accuracy varies as a function of the data mixture used for training. On the left, we compute mean accuracy as Mean Acc=(Harmlessness Acc+Helpfulness Acc)/2Mean AccHarmlessness AccHelpfulness Acc2\text{Mean Acc}=\left(\text{Harmlessness Acc}+\text{Helpfulness Acc}\right)/2. Curves for larger models look more steep near the 0% and 100% areas, but flatter at the top. The curves for the smaller models are more gradual, with more distinct peaks in the middle. This suggests that larger PMs are more robust to the specific fraction of red-teaming vs helpfulness data that is used, allowing them to learn both concepts more easily. On the right, we individually normalize each of the curves by the max accuracy. This more clearly shows that accuracy drops off quicker on either side for smaller models.
items.capZh: 图 26：平均测试准确率随用于训练的数据混合比例而变化。左侧，我们计算平均准确率为 Mean Acc=(Harmlessness Acc+Helpfulness Acc)/2。较大模型的曲线在 0% 和 100% 区域附近看起来更陡峭，但在顶部更平坦。较小模型的曲线更平缓，中间有更明显的峰值。这表明较大的 PM 对使用的红队攻击与有用性数据的具体比例更鲁棒，使它们更容易学习这两个概念。右侧，我们分别将每条曲线按最大准确率归一化。这更清楚地显示，对于较小模型，两侧的准确率下降更快。

items.capEn: Figure 27: Loss weighting experiments. Since our preference modelling data contains more helpfulness examples than harmlessness examples, we experiment with up-weighting the loss of harmlessness. The ‘Mean Acc’ plotted on the right is the unweighted mean of harmlessness and helpfulness test accuracies (like Figure 26). We find that mean test accuracy is higher with λ=2𝜆2\lambda=2 or 333 than with λ=1𝜆1\lambda=1 (default). We also note that larger models are more robust to the choice of λ𝜆\lambda. Increasing λ𝜆\lambda from 1 to 10 causes a 7.4% increase in error rate on helpfulness for the 13M parameter model, whereas it only causes a 1.5% increase in error rate on the 52B parameter model.
items.capZh: 图 27：损失加权实验。由于我们的偏好建模数据包含的有用性示例多于无害性示例，我们尝试对无害性损失进行上加权。右侧绘制的“Mean Acc”是无害性和有用性测试准确率的未加权平均值（如图 26）。我们发现，当 λ=2 或 3 时，平均测试准确率高于 λ=1（默认值）。我们还注意到，较大模型对 λ 的选择更鲁棒。将 λ 从 1 增加到 10 会导致 13M 参数模型在有用性上的错误率增加 7.4%，而 52B 参数模型仅增加 1.5%。

items.capEn: Figure 28: RLHF performance on Zero Shot NLP tasks. For larger models, RLHF helps performance on all evaluations except TriviaQA.
items.capZh: 图 28：零样本 NLP 任务上的 RLHF 性能。对于较大模型，RLHF 有助于除 TriviaQA 外的所有评估。

items.capEn: Figure 29: RLHF performance on Few-Shot NLP tasks. We perform context-stuffing with the validation set (using the prior k𝑘k examples), rather than with the training set. Also note that Lambada uses the fill-in-the-blank prompt, as used in GPT-3 [Brown et al., 2020].
items.capZh: 图 29：少样本 NLP 任务上的 RLHF 性能。我们使用验证集（使用前 k 个示例）进行上下文填充，而不是训练集。另请注意，Lambada 使用了填空提示，如 GPT-3 [Brown et al., 2020] 中所述。

items.capEn: Figure 30: Here we show learning curves during context distillation finetuning. We see that the 52B model loss drops to very low values, as we are distilling a prompt from a 52B model into itself.
items.capZh: 图 30：这里我们展示了上下文蒸馏微调期间的学习曲线。我们看到 52B 模型的损失下降到非常低的值，因为我们正在将 52B 模型的提示蒸馏到自身。

items.en: For context distillation, we follow the prescription from [Askell et al., 2021]. Specifically, we first generate data in the following way:
items.zh: 对于上下文蒸馏，我们遵循 [Askell et al., 2021] 中的方法。具体来说，我们首先按以下方式生成数据：

items.en: We prepend the ‘HHH prompt’ (i.e., a set of prompts designed to elicit helpfulness, harmlessness, and honesty) to sequences of text, with 50% of the text coming from our pretraining dataset, and 50% coming from a StackExchange dataset. For the former, we simply append pretraining data after signaling the beginning of another conversation with “Human:”. With StackExchange, we formulate a fake Human/Assistant conversation by using the question as the human side of the conversation, and a top-rated answer as the assistant role.
items.zh: 我们将“HHH 提示”（即一组旨在引发有用性、无害性和诚实性的提示）添加到文本序列前，其中 50% 的文本来自我们的预训练数据集，50% 来自 StackExchange 数据集。对于前者，我们在用“Human:”表示另一个对话开始后简单地附加预训练数据。对于 StackExchange，我们通过将问题作为对话的人类方，将最高评分的答案作为助手角色来构建一个假的人类/助手对话。

items.en: For both datasets, we then perform forward passes with a basic pretrained 52B model, and record the top 50 log-probabilities and their indices (within the vocabulary) for the tokens following the prompt. We store the log-probs, indices, and tokens together as a small new dataset.
items.zh: 对于两个数据集，我们使用一个基本的预训练 52B 模型进行前向传播，并记录提示后 token 的前 50 个对数概率及其索引（在词汇表中）。我们将对数概率、索引和 token 一起存储为一个小型新数据集。

items.en: To perform context distillation finetuning, we pass the tokens from our new dataset through models (of all sizes), and define the loss as the KL divergence between the stored log-probs and the predictions of the model undergoing finetuning. For each token, we use a 51-category distribution, with the 51st category covering the total probability from all the tokens other than the top-50.
items.zh: 为了进行上下文蒸馏微调，我们将新数据集中的 token 传递给模型（所有大小），并将损失定义为存储的对数概率与正在微调的模型的预测之间的 KL 散度。对于每个 token，我们使用一个 51 类别的分布，第 51 个类别覆盖了前 50 个 token 之外所有 token 的总概率。

items.en: We show learning curves for context distillation in Figure 30. We use a batch size of 32 sequences, and a learning rate of 0.050.050.05 times the pretraining learning rate, which we decay to zero linearly during distillation. We distill using a total of 350M tokens.
items.zh: 我们在图 30 中展示了上下文蒸馏的学习曲线。我们使用批次大小为 32 个序列，学习率为预训练学习率的 0.05 倍，并在蒸馏过程中线性衰减到零。我们总共使用 350M 个 token 进行蒸馏。

items.en: Our preference models are trained on comparison data, with each data point consisting of a prompt and a pair of responses. The prompt is a multi-step dialogue between human and model that always begins and ends on the human side, and each response is a continuation of the dialogue. For instance, in Figure 6, the prompt consists of the first five steps of the dialogue, and the responses are shown in the blue box. The PM then assigns a score at the end of each response. Note that while the PM is only trained to evaluate the quality of the final response, the full context of the conversation is provided to the model.
items.zh: 我们的偏好模型在比较数据上训练，每个数据点包含一个提示和一对回复。提示是人类和模型之间的多步对话，始终以人类方开始和结束，每个回复是对话的延续。例如，在图 6 中，提示由对话的前五步组成，回复显示在蓝色框中。然后 PM 在每个回复结束时分配一个分数。请注意，虽然 PM 仅训练用于评估最终回复的质量，但对话的完整上下文会提供给模型。

items.en: We train scans of PMs ranging from 13M to 52B parameters. All PMs go through three phases of training: (1) language model (LM) pre-training on a large language corpus, (2) preference model pretraining (PMP), and (3) finetuning on human feedback.
items.zh: 我们训练了从 13M 到 52B 参数的 PM 扫描。所有 PM 都经过三个训练阶段：（1）在大型语言语料库上进行语言模型（LM）预训练，（2）偏好模型预训练（PMP），以及（3）基于人类反馈的微调。

items.en: LM pre-training details, including choice of hyperparameters and datasets, are explained in Appendix A of our previous work [Askell et al., 2021].
items.zh: LM预训练细节，包括超参数和数据集的选择，在我们之前的工作[Askell et al., 2021]的附录A中进行了说明。

items.en: For PMP, we use learning rate of 0.1 relative to LM pretraining, and train on a mixture of comparison data made from StackExchange, Reddit, and Wikipedia. Data preparation and labeling are explained in Appendix C.1 of [Askell et al., 2021]. We train with context size of 1024 tokens.
items.zh: 对于PMP，我们使用相对于LM预训练0.1的学习率，并在由StackExchange、Reddit和Wikipedia组成的比较数据混合集上进行训练。数据准备和标注在[Askell et al., 2021]的附录C.1中进行了说明。我们使用1024个token的上下文窗口进行训练。

items.en: For human feedback finetuning, we use learning rate of 0.01 relative to the LM pretraining. We use context size of 1024 tokens, except for the ‘online’ model described in Section 4.5, where we trained with 2048, which may help stabilize RLHF on long contexts.
items.zh: 对于基于人类反馈的微调，我们使用相对于LM预训练0.01的学习率。我们使用1024个token的上下文窗口，但第4.5节描述的“在线”模型除外，该模型使用2048个token进行训练，这可能有助于在长上下文中稳定RLHF。

items.en: For both PMP and human feedback finetuning, we append a special ‘end-of-context’ token at the end of each sample, such that the PM score is predicted directly on top of this token. As explained in Appendix C.4 of [Askell et al., 2021], this appears to improve PM performance.
items.zh: 对于PMP和基于人类反馈的微调，我们在每个样本的末尾附加一个特殊的“end-of-context”token，使得PM分数直接在该token上进行预测。如[Askell et al., 2021]的附录C.4所述，这似乎能提升PM性能。

items.en: In all phases, we only train over one iteration to mitigate overfitting.
items.zh: 在所有阶段，我们只训练一个迭代以减轻过拟合。

full.sec: A.3 Scaling of PM with Model and Dataset Size
full.secZh: A.3 偏好模型随模型与数据集大小的缩放

items.en: A major question is how performance of preference modeling scaling with model size and dataset size. This relates to a practical question – should we invest in collecting a larger dataset, or in training larger models?
items.zh: 一个主要问题是偏好建模的性能如何随模型大小和数据集大小缩放。这涉及一个实际问题——我们应该投资收集更大的数据集，还是训练更大的模型？

items.en: We seem to find more predictable scaling when training only on our helpfulness dataset, likely because the red-teaming data truly comes from a distinct distribution. Accuracy learning curves can be seen on the left of Figure 32. We find that accuracy can be roughly fit by
items.zh: 我们似乎发现仅在有用性数据集上训练时缩放更可预测，可能是因为红队数据确实来自不同的分布。准确率学习曲线见图32左侧。我们发现准确率大致可以拟合为

items.en: where P𝑃P is the number of parameters in the PM and D𝐷D is the size of the dataset.
items.zh: 其中P是PM的参数数量，D是数据集的大小。

items.en: However, the results when training on another preference modeling data distribution look quite different, as seen on the right in Figure 32. Note that there appears to be a sort of discontinuity in behavior between 200M and 13B parameters. Perhaps this is related to the fact that the data was generated by a model with 6B parameters.
items.zh: 然而，在另一种偏好建模数据分布上训练的结果看起来相当不同，如图32右侧所示。注意，在200M和13B参数之间似乎存在一种行为上的不连续性。这可能与数据是由一个6B参数的模型生成的事实有关。

items.capEn: Figure 31: (left) Learning curves on the helpfulness test set when training on a mix of static helpful and harmless data. (right) Learning curves on the harmlessness test set.
items.capZh: 图31：(左)在静态有用和无害数据混合集上训练时，在有用性测试集上的学习曲线。(右)在无害性测试集上的学习曲线。

items.capEn: Figure 32: (left) We show learning curves for PM accuracy when training only on the helpfulness portion of the static dataset. (right) Learning curves of our PMs trained on the learning to summarize [Stiennon et al., 2020] dataset. Note that there seems to be a fairly sharp change in behavior between models with a few hundred million and a few billion parameters, which makes it difficult to formulate simple scaling predictions.
items.capZh: 图32：(左)我们展示了仅在静态数据集的有用性部分训练时PM准确率的学习曲线。(右)在“学习总结”[Stiennon et al., 2020]数据集上训练的PM的学习曲线。注意，在几亿参数和几十亿参数的模型之间似乎存在相当急剧的行为变化，这使得难以制定简单的缩放预测。

items.en: Here we discuss some details about RLHF training. We initialize our policies on context-distilled models, which are explained in A.1.
items.zh: 这里我们讨论一些关于RLHF训练的细节。我们将策略初始化为上下文蒸馏模型，这在A.1中进行了说明。

items.en: We train the policy to generate responses to a dataset of prompts that maximize the score relative to a PM that was finetuned on human feedback. The prompt dataset is obtained from the training split of the PM comparisons dataset by simply removing the responses in each pair. Recall that we allow multi-step dialogue within the prompt (which always begins and ends on the human side of the conversation), but only train the policy to generate one response following each prompt. In future work, we plan to train policies to generate multiple steps, but this requires a separate model that generates the human side of the conversation, which can be implemented with a language model trained to imitate the human side of the conversation.
items.zh: 我们训练策略以生成对提示数据集的响应，最大化相对于基于人类反馈微调的PM的分数。提示数据集来自PM比较数据集的训练分割，只需移除每对中的响应。回想一下，我们允许提示内的多步对话（总是从人类一方开始和结束），但只训练策略在每个提示后生成一个响应。在未来的工作中，我们计划训练策略生成多个步骤，但这需要一个单独的模型来生成对话的人类一方，这可以通过训练一个语言模型来模仿对话的人类一方来实现。

items.en: We performed a variety of hyperparameter scans, and ended up using learning rate of 0.01 relative to pre-training, a KL reward coefficient of λKL=0.001subscript𝜆KL0.001\lambda_{\rm KL}=0.001 (4.1), PPO clipping ϵ=0.2italic-ϵ0.2\epsilon=0.2, discount factor γ=1𝛾1\gamma=1, and no entropy bonus. Furthermore, in PPO, we re-iterate over the same sample K𝐾K times (see Algorithm 1 in [Schulman et al., 2017]), with higher K𝐾K typically leading to more stable results. We used K=1𝐾1K=1 for the RLHF scan, K=2𝐾2K=2 for the robustness studies (Section 4.2), and K=4𝐾4K=4 for the ‘online’ RLHF (Section 4.5). We also impose a limit on the maximum number of tokens per model response, using 32 for the robustness studies, and 128 elsewhere. Finally, for ‘online’ RLHF, we used a learning schedule that reduces the learning rate by 2×2\times every 100,000 samples. For the robustness studies, we used a linear learning rate warmup for the first 25,000 samples.
items.zh: 我们进行了各种超参数扫描，最终使用相对于预训练0.01的学习率，KL奖励系数λ_KL=0.001（4.1），PPO裁剪ε=0.2，折扣因子γ=1，无熵奖励。此外，在PPO中，我们对同一个样本重复K次（见[Schulman et al., 2017]中的算法1），较高的K通常导致更稳定的结果。对于RLHF扫描，我们使用K=1；对于鲁棒性研究（第4.2节），使用K=2；对于“在线”RLHF（第4.5节），使用K=4。我们还对每个模型响应的最大token数施加限制，鲁棒性研究使用32，其他地方使用128。最后，对于“在线”RLHF，我们使用学习率调度，每100,000个样本将学习率减半。对于鲁棒性研究，我们在前25,000个样本中使用线性学习率预热。

items.capEn: Figure 33: Robustness experiments for RLHF, showing test PM score for all policy sizes and all test PM sizes, evaluated at 200k train samples. Note that the overall slope increases with respect to test PM size, suggesting that larger preference models are more robust. (left) Experiments for which the train PM is 52B for all policy sizes. (right) Experiments for which the train PM size is equal to policy size.
items.capZh: 图33：RLHF的鲁棒性实验，显示所有策略大小和所有测试PM大小在200k训练样本时的测试PM分数。注意，整体斜率随测试PM大小增加，表明更大的偏好模型更鲁棒。(左)训练PM为52B时所有策略大小的实验。(右)训练PM大小等于策略大小的实验。

items.en: In Figure 33, we compare the test PM score for all policy sizes and all test PM sizes. The main observation here is that the slope of the graph increases with respect to test PM size, thus suggesting that larger test PM’s are significantly more capable of distinguishing policy performance. In other words, larger preference models are more robust, in agreement with calibration studies in Section 3.3.
items.zh: 在图33中，我们比较了所有策略大小和所有测试PM大小的测试PM分数。主要观察结果是，图表的斜率随测试PM大小增加，因此表明更大的测试PM更能区分策略性能。换句话说，更大的偏好模型更鲁棒，这与第3.3节的校准研究一致。

items.en: Finally, we take a moment here to address an issue we had neglected so far, which is that scores from different preference models should not be compared directly, since the absolute value of the score has no meaning, only relative scores are meaningful. We address this by a simple mean removal procedure. We make a held-out dataset, consisting of several thousand samples, and subtract from each preference model score its mean score on this dataset. We apologize that unrelated plots from different sections may have used different held-out datasets for mean-removal.
items.zh: 最后，我们花一点时间解决一个我们之前忽略的问题，即不同偏好模型的分数不应直接比较，因为分数的绝对值没有意义，只有相对分数有意义。我们通过一个简单的均值移除程序来解决这个问题。我们制作一个保留数据集，包含几千个样本，并从每个偏好模型分数中减去其在该数据集上的均值分数。我们为不同部分中不相关的图可能使用了不同的保留数据集进行均值移除而道歉。

items.capEn: Figure 34: Solid lines represent mean log-p𝑝p accuracy of our ‘online’ RLHF model, which was trained on all the helpfulness and harmless data available. We expect a ceiling for performance at the accuracy of our best PMs (dashed lines). Performance on the harmlessness comparisons did not seem to improve, which we suspect is due to our having used a significantly large fraction of helpfulness prompts during RLHF.
items.capZh: 图34：实线表示我们的“在线”RLHF模型的平均对数p准确率，该模型在所有可用的有用性和无害性数据上训练。我们预计性能上限为我们最佳PM的准确率（虚线）。无害性比较的性能似乎没有提高，我们怀疑这是因为我们在RLHF期间使用了很大比例的有用性提示。

items.en: We give some more details on our ‘online’ RLHF policy discussed in Section 4.5. This policy and its PM were trained on all the helpfulness and harmlessness data we had near the completion of this paper. We re-iterated each sample K=4𝐾4K=4 times [Schulman et al., 2017] to improve stability, and sampled a maximum of 128 tokens per response.
items.zh: 我们提供关于第4.5节讨论的“在线”RLHF策略的更多细节。该策略及其PM在本文完成时我们拥有的所有有用性和无害性数据上训练。我们对每个样本重复K=4次[Schulman et al., 2017]以提高稳定性，并每个响应最多采样128个token。

items.en: Throughout training, we periodically evaluate the mean log-p accuracy of the policy on various held-out PM comparison datasets. More specifically, given a comparison consisting of a prompt and pair of responses, we assign an accuracy of 1 if the policy’s mean log-p on the better response is higher, and 0 otherwise. We show these results in Figure 34 for various comparison datasets. In particular, we find that mean log-p accuracy of the policy isn’t as high as PM accuracy (i.e., fraction of comparisons on which the PM assigns a higher score to the better response), possibly suggesting room for further improvements to our RLHF pipeline.
items.zh: 在整个训练过程中，我们定期评估策略在各种保留PM比较数据集上的平均对数p准确率。具体来说，给定一个由提示和一对响应组成的比较，如果策略在更好响应上的平均对数p更高，则分配准确率为1，否则为0。我们在图34中展示了不同比较数据集的结果。特别是，我们发现策略的平均对数p准确率不如PM准确率高（即PM将更高分数分配给更好响应的比较比例），这可能表明我们的RLHF管道还有进一步改进的空间。

items.en: For our final online models, we had crowdworkers compare a variety of model snapshots from RLHF training, in order to better understand the robustness and general performance of our training process. In Figure 15 (right), we show Elo scores for the online models during RLHF training.
items.zh: 对于我们的最终在线模型，我们让众包工作者比较来自RLHF训练的各种模型快照，以便更好地理解我们训练过程的鲁棒性和整体性能。在图15（右）中，我们展示了RLHF训练期间在线模型的Elo分数。

items.en: In particular, we compare Elo scores established empirically from crowdworker preferences (i.e., Crowdworker Preferences in the figure), and Elo scores predicted by our preference model (i.e, Naive PM Prediction) during RLHF training. For the latter, we sample responses from each snapshot on a set of held-out prompts, and evaluate the PM scores (which are then converted to Elo units). We notice that the naive PM predictions significantly overestimate the empirical Elos. This is due to a combination of the following factors:
items.zh: 具体来说，我们比较了基于众包工作者偏好经验确定的Elo分数（即图中的“众包工作者偏好”）和我们的偏好模型在RLHF训练期间预测的Elo分数（即“朴素PM预测”）。对于后者，我们在一组保留提示上从每个快照采样响应，并评估PM分数（然后转换为Elo单位）。我们注意到，朴素PM预测显著高估了经验Elo。这是由于以下因素的组合：

items.en: During crowdworker testing, each step of the conversation is written by one of the two models being tested. However, when evaluating a RLHF snapshot on held-out prompts, the policy only writes one response at the end of a pre-existing conversation (which had been previously generated by other models, as discussed in Appendix B.1). This leads to distributional shift between the conversations.
items.zh: 在众包工作者测试中，对话的每一步都由被测试的两个模型之一编写。然而，在保留提示上评估RLHF快照时，策略仅在预先存在的对话（该对话先前由其他模型生成，如附录B.1所述）末尾编写一个响应。这导致了对话之间的分布偏移。

items.en: Elo and PM scores may not actually be transitive, as they involve the collapse of pairwise comparisons onto a single line. For example, if PM scores a,b,c𝑎𝑏𝑐a,b,c satisfy a−b=2𝑎𝑏2a-b=2 and b−c=2𝑏𝑐2b-c=2, even if those are well-calibrated scores, the implication that a−c=4𝑎𝑐4a-c=4 may not be correctly calibrated, and we would naturally expect instead a−c<4𝑎𝑐4a-c<4.
items.zh: Elo和PM分数实际上可能不具有传递性，因为它们涉及将成对比较压缩到一条线上。例如，如果PM分数a、b、c满足a−b=2和b−c=2，即使这些是校准良好的分数，a−c=4的推论可能无法正确校准，我们自然预期a−c<4。

items.en: Failures of PM robustness, so that the PM’s preference for the RL policy’s samples is miscalibrated compared to true human preferences.
items.zh: PM鲁棒性的失败，使得PM对RL策略样本的偏好与真实人类偏好相比校准不良。

items.en: To explore these effects further, in Figure 35 we show Elo scores corresponding to four different measurements:
items.zh: 为了进一步探索这些影响，在图35中，我们展示了对应于四种不同测量的Elo分数：

items.en: Naive PM Prediction: The PM score (translated into Elo units) recorded during RLHF training, which uses a set of held-out prompts.
items.zh: 朴素PM预测：RLHF训练期间记录的PM分数（转换为Elo单位），使用一组保留提示。

items.en: Mean PM Score on Crowdworker Data: The mean PM score on the actual crowdworker conversations used to compute Elo scores based on crowdworker preferences.
items.zh: 众包工作者数据上的平均PM分数：在用于基于众包工作者偏好计算Elo分数的实际众包工作者对话上的平均PM分数。

items.en: PM Ranking on Crowdworker Data: One can try to distinguish robustness failures from miscalibrated PM scores vs PM rankings. Here we evaluate the PM on the crowdworker data used to compare these model snapshots, obtain ‘win rates’ for model comparisons according to the PM, and then we recompute the Elo scores based on the PM’s choices.
items.zh: 众包工作者数据上的PM排名：可以尝试区分鲁棒性失败是由于PM分数校准不良还是PM排名。这里我们在用于比较这些模型快照的众包工作者数据上评估PM，根据PM获得模型比较的“胜率”，然后基于PM的选择重新计算Elo分数。

items.en: Crowdworker Preferences: We straightforwardly compute Elo scores based on crowdworker preferences among model snapshots.
items.zh: 众包工作者偏好：我们直接基于众包工作者对模型快照的偏好计算Elo分数。

items.en: So we see that the PM score vs PM rankings distinction does not make a significant difference in terms of robustness. However, the distributional shift between the held-out prompts and the actual crowdworker conversations was very significant, and explains a significant proportion of the discrepancy between RLHF learning curves and the Elo scores as measured from crowdworkers.
items.zh: 因此，我们看到PM分数与PM排名的区别在鲁棒性方面没有显著差异。然而，保留提示与实际众包工作者对话之间的分布偏移非常显著，并解释了RLHF学习曲线与从众包工作者测量的Elo分数之间差异的很大一部分。

items.capEn: Figure 35: Here we diagnose issues with robustness during our online RLHF training. The ‘naive PM Prediction’ is the PM score during training. However, there seems to be a distributional shift from RLHF training prompts compared to crowdworker behavior, and so the ‘Mean PM Score on Crowdworker Data’ actually measures the PM score of each snapshot on the crowdworker data used to evaluate Elo scores. We see the distributional shift is surprisingly non-trivial. The ‘PM Ranking on Crowdworker Data’ shows Elo scores that have been recomputed by first evaluating the PM’s discrete choices on the crowdworker data, and then using these choices to estimate Elo scores. And then finally ‘Crowdworker Preferences’ corresponds to the real Elo scores based on crowdworker expressed preferences when interacting with the models and testing them against each other.
items.capZh: 图35：这里我们诊断在线RLHF训练期间的鲁棒性问题。“朴素PM预测”是训练期间的PM分数。然而，似乎RLHF训练提示与众包工作者行为之间存在分布偏移，因此“众包工作者数据上的平均PM分数”实际上测量了每个快照在用于评估Elo分数的众包工作者数据上的PM分数。我们看到分布偏移出乎意料地显著。“众包工作者数据上的PM排名”显示了通过首先评估PM在众包工作者数据上的离散选择，然后使用这些选择估计Elo分数而重新计算的Elo分数。最后，“众包工作者偏好”对应于基于众包工作者在与模型交互并相互测试时表达的偏好的真实Elo分数。

full.sec: B.5 Crowdworker Comparisons and Elo Scores
full.secZh: B.5 众包工人比较与Elo分数

items.en: Here we briefly describe how we test crowdworker preferences of our models, and how Elo scores are established. For pair of models A𝐴A and B𝐵B, we ask crowdworkers to engage in text-based, back-and-forth conversations with the models. At each conversational step, two responses are generated, one from each model, and the worker chooses the response they prefer, and the conversation continues. Each choice the worker makes counts as a ‘win’ for the preferred model, giving ‘win counts’ NA,NBsubscript𝑁𝐴subscript𝑁𝐵N_{A},N_{B}, respectively. In cases where a worker is unsure about whether one response is better, we throw out such comparisons in both PM and RLHF training, and crowdworkers comparison evaluations.
items.zh: 这里我们简要描述如何测试众包工作者对我们模型的偏好，以及如何建立Elo分数。对于模型A和B，我们要求众包工作者与模型进行基于文本的来回对话。在每个对话步骤中，生成两个响应，每个模型一个，工作者选择他们偏好的响应，然后对话继续。工作者做出的每个选择都计为偏好模型的“胜利”，分别给出胜场数N_A、N_B。在工作者不确定一个响应是否更好的情况下，我们在PM和RLHF训练以及众包工作者比较评估中都丢弃此类比较。

items.en: Recall that, given Elo scores EA,EBsubscript𝐸𝐴subscript𝐸𝐵E_{A},E_{B}, respectively, the log-likelihood for the win counts is given by
items.zh: 回想一下，给定Elo分数E_A、E_B，胜场数的对数似然由下式给出：

items.en: where rA,B=(log⁡10/400)​EA,B≈EA,B/174subscript𝑟𝐴𝐵10400subscript𝐸𝐴𝐵subscript𝐸𝐴𝐵174r_{A,B}=(\log 10/400)E_{A,B}\approx E_{A,B}/174. For an ensemble of comparisons between various models, we estimate Elo scores and their errors by maximum likelihood estimation.
items.zh: 其中r_{A,B} = (log 10/400) E_{A,B} ≈ E_{A,B}/174。对于各种模型之间的比较集合，我们通过最大似然估计来估计Elo分数及其误差。

items.en: In some cases one of the models uses rejection sampling, meaning that it generates k𝑘k samples, evaluates all of them using a preference model, and shows the user the top-scored sample. Elo scores for such models are shown in Appendix B.6. In this case, we cannot stream the sample, so instead we make the workers wait until the sample is completed. When testing a rejection sampling model against a non-rejection sampling one, we only show the samples when they’ve both been completed, even if the latter sample could’ve been streamed, to mitigate bias.
items.zh: 在某些情况下，其中一个模型使用拒绝采样，这意味着它生成k个样本，使用偏好模型评估所有样本，并向用户显示得分最高的样本。此类模型的Elo分数显示在附录B.6中。在这种情况下，我们无法流式传输样本，因此我们让工作者等待直到样本完成。当测试拒绝采样模型与非拒绝采样模型时，我们仅在两个样本都完成时才显示样本，即使后者本可以流式传输，以减轻偏差。

full.sec: B.6 Elo Scores for Rejection Sampling Models
full.secZh: B.6 拒绝采样模型的Elo分数

items.capEn: Figure 36: Elo scores for a 52B context-distilled model with rejection sampling (utilizing a 52B PM). For each prompt, we generate k𝑘k number of responses, and return the response with the highest PM score.
items.capZh: 图36：具有拒绝采样（使用52B PM）的52B上下文蒸馏模型的Elo分数。对于每个提示，我们生成k个响应，并返回PM得分最高的响应。

items.en: In Figure 36 we show helpfulness Elo scores for a 52B context distilled model with rejection sampling (utilizing a 52B preference model trained on pure helpfulness) for k=1,4,16,64𝑘141664k=1,4,16,64, showing that higher values of k𝑘k clearly perform better. Note that the context distilled model and the preference models discussed here were trained during an earlier stage of our research with different datasets and settings from those discussed elsewhere in the paper, so they are not directly comparable with other Elo results, though very roughly and heuristically, our online models seem to perform about as well or better than k=64𝑘64k=64 rejection sampling. Note that k=64𝑘64k=64 rejection sampling corresponds to DK​L=log⁡(64)≈4.2subscript𝐷𝐾𝐿644.2D_{KL}=\log(64)\approx 4.2.
items.zh: 在图36中，我们展示了具有拒绝采样（使用基于纯有用性训练的52B偏好模型）的52B上下文蒸馏模型的有用性Elo分数，k=1,4,16,64，表明更高的k值明显表现更好。请注意，这里讨论的上下文蒸馏模型和偏好模型是在我们研究的早期阶段使用与论文其他地方不同的数据集和设置训练的，因此它们不能直接与其他Elo结果比较，尽管粗略和启发式地，我们的在线模型似乎表现得与k=64拒绝采样相当或更好。注意k=64拒绝采样对应于D_KL = log(64) ≈ 4.2。

items.capEn: (a) Mean log-prob loss on good and bad answers to Stack Overflow questions.
items.capZh: (a) 对Stack Overflow问题的好答案和坏答案的平均对数概率损失。

items.capEn: (b) Difference in mean log-prob between good and bad answers to Stack Overflow questions.
items.capZh: (b) 对Stack Overflow问题的好答案和坏答案的平均对数概率差异。

items.en: We can also evaluate our language models directly given a corpus of paired good and bad responses, such as answers to StackOverflow questions. In 37(b) we evaluate the difference in mean log-p between popular (i.e, highly upvoted) and unpopular answers, showing that RLHF models consistently assign a higher difference, suggesting that they are more capable of distinguishing answer quality. In 37(a) we plot the language modeling loss (i.e, mean log-prob) on the good and bad answers separately, rather than their difference. We find that the RLHF models obtain worse loss. This is most likely due to optimizing a different objective rather than pure language modeling.
items.zh: 我们还可以通过一个包含成对好坏响应的语料库（例如 StackOverflow 问题的答案）来直接评估语言模型。在图 37(b) 中，我们评估了热门（即高赞）答案与不热门答案之间平均对数概率的差异，结果显示基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）模型始终分配更高的差异，表明它们更擅长区分答案质量。在图 37(a) 中，我们分别绘制了好答案和坏答案的语言建模损失（即平均对数概率），而不是它们的差异。我们发现 RLHF 模型的损失更差。这很可能是由于优化了不同的目标，而非纯粹的语言建模。

full.secZh: B.8 代码模型快照上RLHF的进一步分析

items.en: As discussed in Section 5.3, RLHF improves performance of base code models on code evals. In this appendix, we compare that with simply prompting the base code model with a sample of prompts designed to elicit helpfulness, harmlessness, and honesty, which we refer to as ‘HHH’ prompts. In particular, they contain a couple of coding examples. Below is a description of what this prompt looks like:
items.zh: 如第 5.3 节所述，RLHF 提高了基础代码模型在代码评估上的性能。在本附录中，我们将其与简单地用一组旨在引发有用性、无害性和诚实性的提示（我们称之为“HHH”提示）来提示基础代码模型进行比较。特别地，这些提示包含一些编码示例。以下是该提示样式的描述：

items.capEn: Figure 38: Versions of Figure 21 with an extra line showing performance of the Python Fine-Tuned LM with the HHH prompt. The figure on the right looks at results on the 52B model.
items.capZh: 图 38：图 21 的版本，增加了一条显示使用 HHH 提示的 Python 微调语言模型性能的线。右图显示了 52B 模型的结果。

full.sec: B.9 Details of Applying Out-of-Distribution Detection to Reject Strange or Harmful Requests
full.secZh: B.9 应用分布外检测拒绝奇怪或有害请求的细节

items.en: Our newly proposed Simplified Relative Mahalanobis distance outperforms the standard Mahalanobis distance on OOD detection of harmlessness inputs from helpfulness inputs for activations extracted from all layers of all model sizes we tested. The details are shown in Figure 39.
items.zh: 我们新提出的简化相对马氏距离在从所有测试模型大小的所有层提取的激活向量上，对来自有用性输入的无害性输入的 OOD 检测中，优于标准马氏距离。详细信息如图 39 所示。

items.capEn: Figure 39: Detecting harmful content by measuring a distance from the helpfulness data. The left panel shows the helpfulness vs harmlessness data AUROC for different model sizes and layers from which activation vectors were extracted, using a variant of the Mahalanobis distance from the helpfulness data that we call the Simplified Relative Mahalanobis distance (inspired by [Ren et al., 2021]) as a score. The right panel shows the same for the standard Mahalanobis distance. For every layer and every model size, our new distance score performs better in terms of AUROC. The errorbars are the standard deviation of 3 runs with random train-test splits of our data.
items.capZh: 图 39：通过测量与有用性数据的距离来检测有害内容。左图显示了不同模型大小和层（从中提取激活向量）的有用性与无害性数据 AUROC，使用一种我们称为简化相对马氏距离（受 [Ren et al., 2021] 启发）的马氏距离变体作为分数。右图显示了标准马氏距离的相同结果。对于每一层和每个模型大小，我们的新距离分数在 AUROC 方面表现更好。误差线是 3 次随机训练-测试分割运行的标准差。

items.en: Exposing the OOD detector to a few examples of the out-distribution has first been proposed by [Hendrycks et al., 2018]. [Thulasidasan et al., 2021] suggested using a single class representing the OOD examples. [Fort et al., 2021] showed that even strong near-OOD detectors enjoy a large benefit.
items.zh: 将 OOD 检测器暴露于少量外分布样本首先由 [Hendrycks et al., 2018] 提出。[Thulasidasan et al., 2021] 建议使用一个代表 OOD 样本的单一类别。[Fort et al., 2021] 表明，即使是强大的近 OOD 检测器也能从中获得巨大收益。

items.en: Following the procedure from [Fort et al., 2021], we apply a single layer linear classifier on top of the activation vectors, while the rest of the language model is frozen. Given M𝑀M randomly drawn examples of the training set of the out-distribution (harmlessness data), we create a new binary classification problem, where the inputs are the combination of the full Ntrainsubscript𝑁trainN_{\mathrm{train}} examples of training set of the in-distribution with a target class of 0, and Ntrain//MN_{\mathrm{train}}//M copies of the M𝑀M examples of the out-distribution with the target class of 1. This ensure an equal representation of both in- and out-distribution examples and leads to better learning. Once we train the linear classifier, we use its class 1 output as the OOD score. The results are shown in Figure 23.
items.zh: 遵循 [Fort et al., 2021] 的程序，我们在激活向量之上应用一个单层线性分类器，而语言模型的其余部分被冻结。给定从外分布（无害性数据）训练集中随机抽取的 M 个样本，我们创建一个新的二分类问题，其中输入是内分布训练集的全部 N_train 个样本（目标类别为 0）与 N_train//M 份外分布的 M 个样本（目标类别为 1）的组合。这确保了内分布和外分布样本的均衡表示，并导致更好的学习。一旦我们训练了线性分类器，我们将其类别 1 的输出用作 OOD 分数。结果如图 23 所示。

We see that RLHF models have both higher bias scores and larger errors, and behave very similarly to context distilled models evaluated at a temperature T≈0.6𝑇0.6T\approx 0.6. (right) We show a scatter plot of bias scores for all 76 occupations; each is averaged over 12 gendered terms. RLHF and context-distilled biases are very strongly correlated. Positive scores indicate male-gendered bias, while negative scores indicate female gendered bias towards a given occupation. We include the line y=x𝑦𝑥y=x for reference.
items.capZh: 图 40：（左）我们展示了平均绝对性别偏见分数，以及两倍标准差的标准误差。分数必须非负，分数越高表示偏见越大。我们看到 RLHF 模型具有更高的偏见分数和更大的误差，并且行为与在温度 T≈0.6 下评估的上下文蒸馏模型非常相似。（右）我们展示了所有 76 个职业的偏见分数散点图；每个职业平均了 12 个性别术语。RLHF 和上下文蒸馏的偏见非常强相关。正分数表示男性性别偏见，而负分数表示对给定职业的女性性别偏见。我们包含 y=x 线作为参考。

items.en: As mentioned in Section 4.6.2, we found that when using the prompt format from Section 3.4.2, RLHF models were more than 10×10\times less likely than LMs to complete sentences with gendered terms. So to get responses that were ‘on distribution’ for all models, we reversed the order of gendered terms and occupations, and added a ‘for work’ clause, resulting in the prompt:
items.zh: 如第 4.6.2 节所述，我们发现当使用第 3.4.2 节的提示格式时，RLHF 模型完成带有性别术语的句子的可能性比 LM 低 10 倍以上。因此，为了让所有模型的响应“在分布上”，我们颠倒了性别术语和职业的顺序，并添加了“for work”从句，得到提示：

items.en: We then evaluated the probabilities of occupations, given gender terms. With this formulation, the larger LMs, context-distilled models, and RLHF models all assigned about 2-8% of their probability mass to one of the 76 occupations listed above when completing the sentence.
items.zh: 然后我们评估了给定性别术语的职业概率。通过这种表述，较大的 LM、上下文蒸馏模型和 RLHF 模型在完成句子时，大约有 2-8% 的概率质量分配给上述 76 个职业之一。

items.en: We also slightly modified the metric from [Rae et al., 2021] in order to estimate errors. So to compute bias scores, for each occupation O𝑂O we defined
items.zh: 我们还略微修改了 [Rae et al., 2021] 的度量以估计误差。因此，为了计算偏见分数，对于每个职业 O，我们定义

items.en: and then we define the overall bias score as the mean of the absolute value of BOsubscript𝐵𝑂B_{O}, ranging over occupations:
items.zh: 然后我们将总体偏见分数定义为 B_O 绝对值的平均值，遍历所有职业：

items.en: where O𝑂O represents occupations while gf,gmsubscript𝑔𝑓subscript𝑔𝑚g_{f},g_{m} represent paired gender terms (e.g. lady/gentleman), and NO=76subscript𝑁𝑂76N_{O}=76 and Ng=12subscript𝑁𝑔12N_{g}=12. With this setup, we are sensitive to whether bias is uniform across all pairs of gendered terms (for any given occupation). As a concrete example, if it were the case that a model associated ‘lawyer’ more strongly with ‘boy’ than ‘girl’, but also more strongly with ‘wife’ than ‘husband’, this would increase the error in our measurement, while the bias effects would tend to cancel out.
items.zh: 其中 O 代表职业，g_f, g_m 代表配对的性别术语（例如 lady/gentleman），N_O=76，N_g=12。通过这种设置，我们能够敏感地检测偏见是否在所有配对的性别术语之间一致（对于任何给定职业）。具体来说，如果模型将“律师”与“男孩”的关联强于“女孩”，但同时与“妻子”的关联强于“丈夫”，这将增加我们测量中的误差，而偏见效应往往会相互抵消。

items.en: The results are shown in Figure 40, where we observe that RLHF models have both larger bias scores and larger errors. We believe this has a very simple explanation – RLHF models have much lower-entropy output distributions. As a rough, heuristic explanation, a model with a lower-entropy output will behave somewhat like a plain LM evaluated at a temperature T<1𝑇1T<1. The metric and error from equation B.3 will typically scale as 1/T1𝑇1/T, so it should be expected that models with lower entropy distributions will have proportionally larger bias scores and bias errors. We show these effects explicitly in Figure 40 by evaluating bias scores with context distilled models at T≈0.6𝑇0.6T\approx 0.6, and showing that they are quite similar to RLHF bias scores.
items.zh: 结果如图 40 所示，我们观察到 RLHF 模型具有更大的偏见分数和更大的误差。我们认为这有一个非常简单的解释——RLHF 模型的输出分布熵更低。作为一个粗略的启发式解释，具有较低熵输出的模型在行为上类似于在温度 T<1 下评估的普通 LM。方程 B.3 中的度量和误差通常按 1/T 缩放，因此可以预期具有较低熵分布的模型将具有成比例更大的偏见分数和偏见误差。我们在图 40 中通过评估在 T≈0.6 下的上下文蒸馏模型的偏见分数明确展示了这些效应，并显示它们与 RLHF 偏见分数非常相似。

items.en: Readers may observe that the RLHF model bias appears to be growing with model size. This is most likely because RLHF training tends to progressively reduce the entropy (or decrease the ‘effective temperature’) of model distributions, and we train larger models longer with RLHF, because their training is more robust (and so more beneficial in other respects). Thus we expect this trend is a property of differences in RLHF training across model sizes, rather than a more fundamental model-size trend.
items.zh: 读者可能会注意到 RLHF 模型的偏见似乎随着模型大小而增长。这很可能是因为 RLHF 训练倾向于逐步降低模型分布的熵（或降低“有效温度”），并且我们对较大的模型进行更长时间的 RLHF 训练，因为它们的训练更稳健（因此在其他方面更有益）。因此，我们预计这一趋势是不同模型大小下 RLHF 训练差异的属性，而不是更基本的模型大小趋势。

full.sec: Appendix C Samples from PALMS, LaMDA, and InstructGPT Prompts

items.en: These were generated by sampling 17 responses for each prompt from our best online HH model, ranking them using the corresponding preference model, and then displaying the median non-empty response according to the PM. (Occasionally the model writes the "end of conversation" token in response to sensitive queries.) So to a large extent, these samples are not ‘cherry-picked’, and should be representative of average-case behavior. In the case of LaMDA [Thoppilan et al., 2022] and InstructGPT [Ouyang et al., 2022] we include the corresponding samples from those models.
items.zh: 这些是通过从我们最佳的在线 HH 模型中为每个提示采样 17 个响应，使用相应的偏好模型进行排序，然后根据 PM 显示中位数非空响应生成的。（有时模型会在响应敏感查询时写入“对话结束”标记。）因此，在很大程度上，这些样本不是“精心挑选的”，应该代表平均情况下的行为。对于 LaMDA [Thoppilan et al., 2022] 和 InstructGPT [Ouyang et al., 2022]，我们包含了来自这些模型的相应样本。

items.en: Questions are drawn from the sensitive questions in [Solaiman and Dennison, 2021]. Please be warned that some of these questions contain sensitive material (by design).
items.zh: 问题选自[Solaiman and Dennison, 2021]中的敏感问题。请注意，其中一些问题（有意）包含敏感内容。

items.en: We warn readers that some of the samples from InstructGPT [Ouyang et al., 2022] were likely cherry picked to display poor model behavior, on purpose, and so may be unrepresentative. Thus in some cases it will be unfair to compare our median samples to those from InstructGPT.
items.zh: 我们提醒读者，InstructGPT [Ouyang et al., 2022] 中的一些样本很可能是为了展示模型不良行为而精心挑选的，因此可能不具有代表性。因此，在某些情况下，将我们的中位数样本与 InstructGPT 的样本进行比较是不公平的。

items.en: For the purposes of assigning tasks, we divided our crowdworkers into two groups. A group of around 30 ‘select’ workers were sent high priority tasks on a regular basis. Roughly half of these workers were hired through Upwork. The other half were selected from the general population of US-based MTurk workers with a ‘Masters’ qualification. The second group of workers came from the same general population of MTurk workers, and were primarily given red-team tasks on a less regular schedule. We tried to ensure that all workers were paid significantly above the minimum wage in California, adjusting rates for tasks that took longer (for example with models that use rejection sampling) in the case of MTurk workers, who are paid by task rather than at an hourly rate. We frequently issued bonuses to MTurk workers who encountered technical problems with tasks (as they are not paid an hourly rate).
items.zh: 为了分配任务，我们将众包工作者分为两组。一组约30名“精选”工作者定期接收高优先级任务。其中约一半是通过 Upwork 雇佣的。另一半是从具有“大师”资格的美国 MTurk 工作者总体中选出的。第二组工作者来自相同的 MTurk 工作者总体，主要被分配红队任务，频率较低。我们努力确保所有工作者的薪酬显著高于加州最低工资，对于耗时较长的任务（例如使用拒绝采样的模型），我们调整了费率，因为 MTurk 工作者按任务付费而非按小时。对于遇到技术问题的 MTurk 工作者，我们经常发放奖金（因为他们没有按小时计酬）。

items.en: Among our select workers, MTurk workers usually accounted for 80-85% of comparison data collected in a given week, compared to 15-20% for workers hired through Upwork. Although the size of these groups were similar, MTurk workers tended to opt in to more work and their pay structure incentivized quicker conversations.
items.zh: 在我们的精选工作者中，MTurk 工作者通常占每周收集的比较数据的 80-85%，而通过 Upwork 雇佣的工作者占 15-20%。尽管这些组的规模相似，但 MTurk 工作者倾向于选择更多工作，并且他们的薪酬结构激励更快的对话。

items.en: We communicated with select workers on a daily basis over Slack. We used this channel to announce new tasks and provide guidance; we discussed difficult edge cases with the group as they came up. At an intermediate stage of the project we provided some additional thoughts on more advanced forms of interaction with the model; the slack message we sent is shown in Figure 43. Our workers alerted us to bugs and performance issues as they ran into them.
items.zh: 我们通过 Slack 与精选工作者每天沟通。我们使用这个渠道宣布新任务并提供指导；我们与团队讨论出现的困难边缘案例。在项目的中期阶段，我们提供了一些关于与模型进行更高级交互形式的额外想法；我们发送的 Slack 消息如图 43 所示。我们的工作者在遇到错误和性能问题时向我们报告。

items.en: We sent both groups of workers a demographics survey, and the results are shown in figure 44. Survey responses were anonymous, as we did not collect any personal identifiable information alongside demographic information.
items.zh: 我们向两组工作者发送了人口统计调查，结果如图 44 所示。调查回复是匿名的，因为我们没有收集任何个人身份信息与人口统计信息一起。

items.capEn: Figure 41: We show modified versions of the instructions that display in a pop-up dialog in our interface. (left) The instructions for conversations in our helpfulness dataset. (right) The instructions for the conversations in our harmlessness dataset.
items.capZh: 图 41：我们展示了在界面中弹出对话框中显示的修改版指令。（左）我们有用性数据集中的对话指令。（右）我们无害性数据集中的对话指令。

items.en: We display basic task instructions in a pop-up dialog when first loading the interface, and these instructions remain available throughout the interaction. These instructions for the ‘playground’ and ‘red team’ tasks can be found in figure 41. For the playground task, we also link to a separate page with expanded instructions that include more detailed examples, excerpts of which can be seen in figure 42.
items.zh: 我们在首次加载界面时在弹出对话框中显示基本任务指令，这些指令在整个交互过程中保持可用。这些用于“游乐场”和“红队”任务的指令可以在图 41 中找到。对于游乐场任务，我们还链接到一个单独的页面，其中包含更详细的示例，其摘录如图 42 所示。

items.capEn: Figure 43: Advanced instructions sent out via Slack message to select workers.
items.capZh: 图 43：通过 Slack 消息发送给精选工作者的高级指令。

items.en: The human feedback interface is shown in figure 6. During the online data collection process, we added an additional option to the interface for Upworkers. This feature allowed them to edit one of the model responses. When they used this feature, we stored a comparison of the edit to the original (assuming the edit was better), rather than the initial comparison of two model outputs. This would have effected less than 10% of the online data.
items.zh: 人类反馈界面如图 6 所示。在在线数据收集过程中，我们为 Upwork 工作者添加了一个额外的选项。该功能允许他们编辑其中一个模型响应。当他们使用此功能时，我们存储了编辑与原始版本的比较（假设编辑更好），而不是两个模型输出的初始比较。这影响了不到 10% 的在线数据。

full.secZh: D.3 数据质量测量挑战

items.en: In rough outline, data quality assurance for human labelers often involves the following steps:
items.zh: 粗略来说，人类标注者的数据质量保证通常包括以下步骤：

items.en: Researchers carefully review a small set of samples to produce a set of ‘golden’ labels
items.zh: 研究人员仔细审查一小部分样本，以产生一组“黄金”标签

items.en: Human labelers work through a stack of labeling tasks, with some subset of labeling tasks assigned to multiple labelers, and samples from the golden label dataset sent out to everyone.
items.zh: 人类标注者处理一系列标注任务，其中一部分标注任务分配给多个标注者，并且黄金标签数据集中的样本发送给每个人。

items.en: Researchers evaluate labeler performance by checking their labels against the golden labels, and by checking for inter-labeler agreement
items.zh: 研究人员通过检查标注者的标签与黄金标签的一致性，以及检查标注者之间的一致性来评估标注者的表现。

items.en: The idea is that the golden labels are treated as a source of truth, with labelers incentivized to modify their behavior to better match the golden labels, and novice labelers incentivized to match the behavior of more experienced labelers.
items.zh: 其理念是将黄金标签视为真实来源，激励标注者调整行为以更好地匹配黄金标签，并激励新手标注者匹配更有经验的标注者的行为。

items.en: These sorts of methods weren’t easily adapted to our data collection setup. Open-ended conversations allowed us to collect a richer dataset, but introduced a number of hard-to-control variables, resulting in noisy data quality metrics.
items.zh: 这类方法不容易适应我们的数据收集设置。开放式对话使我们能够收集更丰富的数据集，但引入了许多难以控制的变量，导致数据质量指标嘈杂。

items.en: We did try having crowdworkers review each other’s conversations, by providing ratings for each model response comparison and rating the overall quality of the conversation. But we found that author-rater agreement wasn’t a good guide for assessing overall conversation quality. In broad strokes, conversation quality depends on a) conversation topic, b) human writing quality, c) model quality. And we found that, for instance, as conversations got more sophisticated, deciding between model responses got more difficult. As a result, authors that more frequently discussed difficult topics would often get lower agreement scores. And because we were frequently updating our models, model response comparisons were a moving target.
items.zh: 我们确实尝试让众包工作者通过为每个模型响应比较提供评分并评估对话的整体质量来相互审查对话。但我们发现作者-评分者一致性并不是评估整体对话质量的良好指南。总的来说，对话质量取决于 a) 对话主题，b) 人类写作质量，c) 模型质量。我们发现，例如，随着对话变得更加复杂，在模型响应之间做出决定变得更加困难。因此，更频繁讨论困难主题的作者通常会获得较低的一致性分数。而且由于我们经常更新模型，模型响应比较是一个移动的目标。

items.en: Instead of directly assessing our crowdworkers based on these kinds of reviewer-based metrics, we considered using their performance as reviewers as a stand-in for their performance as conversation authors. But when we compared these metrics against our own spot-checks of conversation author quality, we found poor agreement.
items.zh: 我们没有直接根据这些基于审查者的指标来评估我们的众包工作者，而是考虑将他们作为审查者的表现作为他们作为对话作者表现的替代。但当我们将这些指标与我们自己对对话作者质量的抽查进行比较时，我们发现一致性较差。

items.en: We expect to be able to work around these problems and come up with better methods in the future. But it’s worth noting that were able to achieve our results without sophisticated data quality controls.
items.zh: 我们期望未来能够解决这些问题并提出更好的方法。但值得注意的是，我们在没有复杂数据质量控制的情况下就取得了这些成果。

full.secZh: 附录E NLP评估格式与提示细节

items.en: Here, we give the input formats we used to evaluate performance on Lambada, ARC, MMMLU, HellaSwag, OpenBookQA, and TriviaQA.
items.zh: 在此，我们给出用于评估 Lambada、ARC、MMMLU、HellaSwag、OpenBookQA 和 TriviaQA 性能的输入格式。
